95	def doctor(
96	    verbose: bool = typer.Option(False, "--verbose", "-v", help="Show detailed diagnostics"),
97	) -> None:
98	    """Diagnose environment issues."""
99	    console.print(f"\n[bold]{t('doctor_title')}[/bold]\n")
100	
101	    issues_found = False
102	    checks = []
103	
104	    # 1. Python version
105	    python_version = platform.python_version()
106	    python_ok = _check_python_version(python_version)
107	    checks.append(
108	        {
109	            "name": t("doctor_check_python"),
110	            "status": "ok" if python_ok else "error",
111	            "value": python_version,
112	            "hint": "Python 3.10+ required" if not python_ok else None,
113	        }
114	    )
115	    if not python_ok:
116	        issues_found = True
117	
118	    # 2. CUDA availability
119	    cuda_version = detect_cuda_version()
120	    checks.append(
121	        {
122	            "name": t("doctor_check_cuda"),
123	            "status": "ok" if cuda_version else "warning",
124	            "value": cuda_version or t("version_cuda_not_found"),
125	            "hint": "CUDA is optional but recommended for GPU acceleration" if not cuda_version else None,
126	        }
127	    )
128	
129	    # 3. GPU detection
130	    gpus = detect_gpus()
131	    if gpus:
132	        gpu_names = ", ".join(g.name for g in gpus)
133	        total_vram = sum(g.vram_gb for g in gpus)
134	        checks.append(
135	            {
136	                "name": t("doctor_check_gpu"),
137	                "status": "ok",
138	                "value": t("doctor_gpu_found", count=len(gpus), names=gpu_names),
139	                "hint": f"Total VRAM: {total_vram}GB",
140	            }
141	        )
142	    else:
143	        checks.append(
144	            {
145	                "name": t("doctor_check_gpu"),
146	                "status": "warning",
147	                "value": t("doctor_gpu_not_found"),
148	                "hint": "GPU recommended for best performance",
149	            }
150	        )
151	
152	    # 4. CPU information
153	    cpu_info = detect_cpu_info()
154	    checks.append(
155	        {
156	            "name": t("doctor_check_cpu"),
157	            "status": "ok",
158	            "value": t("doctor_cpu_info", name=cpu_info.name, cores=cpu_info.cores, threads=cpu_info.threads),
159	            "hint": None,
160	        }
161	    )
162	
163	    # 5. CPU instruction sets (critical for kt-kernel)
164	    isa_list = cpu_info.instruction_sets
165	    # Check for recommended instruction sets
166	    recommended_isa = {"AVX2", "AVX512F", "AMX-INT8"}
167	    has_recommended = bool(set(isa_list) & recommended_isa)
168	    has_avx2 = "AVX2" in isa_list
169	    has_avx512 = any(isa.startswith("AVX512") for isa in isa_list)
170	    has_amx = any(isa.startswith("AMX") for isa in isa_list)
171	
172	    # Determine status and build display string
173	    if has_amx:
174	        isa_status = "ok"
175	        isa_hint = "AMX available - best performance for INT4/INT8"
176	    elif has_avx512:
177	        isa_status = "ok"
178	        isa_hint = "AVX512 available - good performance"
179	    elif has_avx2:
180	        isa_status = "warning"
181	        isa_hint = "AVX2 only - consider upgrading CPU for better performance"
182	    else:
183	        isa_status = "error"
184	        isa_hint = "AVX2 required for kt-kernel"
185	
186	    # Show top instruction sets (prioritize important ones)
187	    display_isa = isa_list[:8] if len(isa_list) > 8 else isa_list
188	    isa_display = ", ".join(display_isa)
189	    if len(isa_list) > 8:
190	        isa_display += f" (+{len(isa_list) - 8} more)"
191	
192	    checks.append(
193	        {
194	            "name": t("doctor_check_cpu_isa"),
195	            "status": isa_status,
196	            "value": isa_display if isa_display else "None detected",
197	            "hint": isa_hint,
198	        }
199	    )
200	
201	    # 6. NUMA topology
202	    numa_detail = []
203	    for node, cpus in sorted(cpu_info.numa_info.items()):
204	        if len(cpus) > 6:
205	            cpu_str = f"{cpus[0]}-{cpus[-1]}"
206	        else:
207	            cpu_str = ",".join(str(c) for c in cpus)
208	        numa_detail.append(f"{node}: {cpu_str}")
209	
210	    numa_value = t("doctor_numa_info", nodes=cpu_info.numa_nodes)
211	    if verbose and numa_detail:
212	        numa_value += " (" + "; ".join(numa_detail) + ")"
213	
214	    checks.append(
215	        {
216	            "name": t("doctor_check_numa"),
217	            "status": "ok",
218	            "value": numa_value,
219	            "hint": f"{cpu_info.threads // cpu_info.numa_nodes} threads per node" if cpu_info.numa_nodes > 1 else None,
220	        }
221	    )
222	
223	    # 6b. kt-kernel installation check
224	    kt_info = _get_kt_kernel_info()
225	
226	    if kt_info["installed"]:
227	        # Build display string for kt-kernel
228	        variant = kt_info["cpu_variant"]
229	        version = kt_info["version"]
230	        available_variants = kt_info["available_variants"]
231	
232	        # Determine status based on CPU variant
233	        if variant == "amx":
234	            kt_status = "ok"
235	            kt_hint = "AMX variant loaded - optimal performance"
236	        elif variant.startswith("avx512"):
237	            kt_status = "ok"
238	            kt_hint = "AVX512 variant loaded - good performance"
239	        elif variant == "avx2":
240	            kt_status = "warning"
241	            kt_hint = "AVX2 variant - consider upgrading CPU for AMX/AVX512"
242	        else:
243	            kt_status = "warning"
244	            kt_hint = f"Unknown variant: {variant}"
245	
246	        kt_value = f"v{version} ({variant.upper()})"
247	        if verbose and available_variants:
248	            kt_value += f" [dim] - available: {', '.join(available_variants)}[/dim]"
249	
250	        checks.append(
251	            {
252	                "name": "kt-kernel",
253	                "status": kt_status,
254	                "value": kt_value,
255	                "hint": kt_hint,
256	            }
257	        )
258	
259	        # Show extension file path in verbose mode
260	        if verbose and kt_info.get("extension_file"):
261	            ext_file = os.path.basename(kt_info["extension_file"])
262	            checks.append(
263	                {
264	                    "name": "  └─ Extension",
265	                    "status": "ok",
266	                    "value": ext_file,
267	                    "hint": None,
268	                }
269	            )
270	
271	        # Show installation path in verbose mode
272	        if verbose and kt_info.get("install_path"):
273	            checks.append(
274	                {
275	                    "name": "  └─ Path",
276	                    "status": "ok",
277	                    "value": kt_info["install_path"],
278	                    "hint": None,
279	                }
280	            )
281	    else:
282	        error_msg = kt_info.get("error", "Not installed")
283	        checks.append(
284	            {
285	                "name": "kt-kernel",
286	                "status": "error",
287	                "value": error_msg,
288	                "hint": "kt-kernel is required - run: pip install kt-kernel",
289	            }
290	        )
291	        issues_found = True
292	
293	    # 7. System memory (with frequency if available)
294	    mem_info = detect_memory_info()
295	    if mem_info.frequency_mhz and mem_info.type:
296	        mem_value = t(
297	            "doctor_memory_freq",
298	            available=f"{mem_info.available_gb}GB",
299	            total=f"{mem_info.total_gb}GB",
300	            freq=mem_info.frequency_mhz,
301	            type=mem_info.type,
302	        )
303	    else:
304	        mem_value = t("doctor_memory_info", available=f"{mem_info.available_gb}GB", total=f"{mem_info.total_gb}GB")
305	
306	    ram_ok = mem_info.total_gb >= 32
307	    checks.append(
308	        {
309	            "name": t("doctor_check_memory"),
310	            "status": "ok" if ram_ok else "warning",
311	            "value": mem_value,
312	            "hint": "32GB+ RAM recommended for large models" if not ram_ok else None,
313	        }
314	    )
315	
316	    # 8. Disk space - check all model paths
317	    settings = get_settings()
318	    model_paths = settings.get_model_paths()
319	
320	    # Check all configured model paths
321	    for i, disk_path in enumerate(model_paths):
322	        available_disk, total_disk = detect_disk_space_gb(str(disk_path))
323	        disk_ok = available_disk >= 100
324	
325	        # For multiple paths, add index to name
326	        path_label = f"Model Path {i+1}" if len(model_paths) > 1 else t("doctor_check_disk")
327	
328	        checks.append(
329	            {
330	                "name": path_label,
331	                "status": "ok" if disk_ok else "warning",
332	                "value": t("doctor_disk_info", available=f"{available_disk}GB", path=str(disk_path)),
333	                "hint": "100GB+ free space recommended for model storage" if not disk_ok else None,
334	            }
335	        )
336	
337	    # 6. Required packages
338	    packages = [
339	        ("kt-kernel", ">=0.4.0", False),  # name, version_req, required
340	        ("sglang", ">=0.4.0", False),
341	        ("torch", ">=2.4.0", True),
342	        ("transformers", ">=4.45.0", True),
343	    ]
344	
345	    package_issues = []
346	    for pkg_name, version_req, required in packages:
347	        version = get_installed_package_version(pkg_name)
348	        if version:
349	            package_issues.append((pkg_name, version, "ok"))
350	        elif required:
351	            package_issues.append((pkg_name, t("version_not_installed"), "error"))
352	            issues_found = True
353	        else:
354	            package_issues.append((pkg_name, t("version_not_installed"), "warning"))
355	
356	    if verbose:
357	        checks.append(
358	            {
359	                "name": t("doctor_check_packages"),
360	                "status": "ok" if not any(p[2] == "error" for p in package_issues) else "error",
361	                "value": f"{sum(1 for p in package_issues if p[2] == 'ok')}/{len(package_issues)} installed",
362	                "packages": package_issues,
363	            }
364	        )
365	
366	    # 7. SGLang installation source check
367	    from kt_kernel.cli.utils.sglang_checker import check_sglang_installation, check_sglang_kt_kernel_support
368	
369	    sglang_info = check_sglang_installation()
370	
371	    if sglang_info["installed"]:
372	        if sglang_info.get("is_kvcache_fork"):
373	            # Package name is sglang-kt — this is definitively the kvcache-ai fork
374	            if sglang_info["from_source"] and sglang_info["git_info"]:
375	                git_remote = sglang_info["git_info"].get("remote", "unknown")
376	                git_branch = sglang_info["git_info"].get("branch", "unknown")
377	                sglang_source_value = f"sglang-kt (Source: {git_remote}, branch: {git_branch})"
378	            elif sglang_info["editable"]:
379	                sglang_source_value = "sglang-kt (editable)"
380	            else:
381	                sglang_source_value = "sglang-kt"
382	            sglang_source_status = "ok"
383	            sglang_source_hint = None
384	        elif sglang_info["from_source"]:
385	            if sglang_info["git_info"]:
386	                git_remote = sglang_info["git_info"].get("remote", "unknown")
387	                git_branch = sglang_info["git_info"].get("branch", "unknown")
388	                sglang_source_value = f"Source (GitHub: {git_remote}, branch: {git_branch})"
389	                sglang_source_status = "ok"
390	                sglang_source_hint = None
391	            else:
392	                sglang_source_value = "Source (editable)"
393	                sglang_source_status = "ok"
394	                sglang_source_hint = None
395	        else:
396	            sglang_source_value = "PyPI sglang (not kvcache-ai fork)"
397	            sglang_source_status = "warning"
398	            sglang_source_hint = t("sglang_pypi_hint")
399	    else:
400	        sglang_source_value = "Not installed"
401	        sglang_source_status = "warning"
402	        sglang_source_hint = t("sglang_install_hint")
403	
404	    checks.append(
405	        {
406	            "name": "SGLang Source",
407	            "status": sglang_source_status,
408	            "value": sglang_source_value,
409	            "hint": sglang_source_hint,
410	        }
411	    )
412	
413	    # 7b. SGLang kt-kernel support check (only if SGLang is installed)
414	    kt_kernel_support = {"supported": True}  # Default to True if not checked
415	    if sglang_info["installed"]:
416	        # Use cache=False to force re-check in doctor, but silent=True since we show in table
417	        kt_kernel_support = check_sglang_kt_kernel_support(use_cache=False, silent=True)
418	
419	        if kt_kernel_support["supported"]:
420	            kt_kernel_value = t("sglang_kt_kernel_supported")
421	            kt_kernel_status = "ok"
422	            kt_kernel_hint = None
423	        else:
424	            kt_kernel_value = t("sglang_kt_kernel_not_supported")
425	            kt_kernel_status = "error"
426	            kt_kernel_hint = "Reinstall SGLang: pip uninstall sglang -y && pip install sglang-kt (or run ./install.sh from ktransformers root)"
427	            issues_found = True
428	
429	        checks.append(
430	            {
431	                "name": "SGLang kt-kernel",
432	                "status": kt_kernel_status,
433	                "value": kt_kernel_value,
434	                "hint": kt_kernel_hint,
435	            }
436	        )
437	
438	    # 8. Potentially conflicting environment variables
439	    # Only surface a row when the variable is actually present; no noise otherwise.
440	    dsv4_submode = os.environ.get("SGLANG_DSV4_2604_SUBMODE")
441	    if dsv4_submode:
442	        checks.append(
443	            {
444	                "name": "Env: SGLANG_DSV4_2604_SUBMODE",
445	                "status": "warning" if dsv4_submode == "2604B" else "ok",
446	                "value": dsv4_submode,
447	                "hint": (
448	                    "Intended for MXFP4 launches only. "
449	                    "Causes a startup crash when kt-method is not MXFP4. Unset it if unused."
450	                    if dsv4_submode == "2604B"
451	                    else None
452	                ),
453	            }
454	        )
455	
456	    # 9. Environment managers
457	    env_managers = detect_env_managers()
458	    docker = check_docker()
459	    env_list = [f"{m.name} {m.version}" for m in env_managers]
460	    if docker:
461	        env_list.append(f"docker {docker.version}")
462	
463	    checks.append(
464	        {
465	            "name": "Environment Managers",
466	            "status": "ok" if env_list else "warning",
467	            "value": ", ".join(env_list) if env_list else "None found",
468	            "hint": "conda or docker recommended for installation" if not env_list else None,
469	        }
470	    )
471	
472	    # Display results
473	    _display_results(checks, verbose)
474	
475	    # Show SGLang installation instructions if not installed
476	    if not sglang_info["installed"]:
477	        from kt_kernel.cli.utils.sglang_checker import print_sglang_install_instructions
478	
479	        console.print()
480	        print_sglang_install_instructions()
481	    # Show kt-kernel installation instructions if SGLang is installed but doesn't support kt-kernel
482	    elif sglang_info["installed"] and not kt_kernel_support.get("supported", True):
483	        from kt_kernel.cli.utils.sglang_checker import print_sglang_kt_kernel_instructions
484	
485	        console.print()
486	        print_sglang_kt_kernel_instructions()
487	
488	    # Summary
489	    console.print()
490	    if issues_found:
491	        print_warning(t("doctor_has_issues"))
492	    else:
493	        print_success(t("doctor_all_ok"))
494	    console.print()
495	
496	
497	def _check_python_version(version: str) -> bool:
498	    """Check if Python version meets requirements."""
499	    parts = version.split(".")
500	    try:
501	        major, minor = int(parts[0]), int(parts[1])
502	        return major >= 3 and minor >= 10
503	    except (IndexError, ValueError):
504	        return False
505	
506	
507	def _display_results(checks: list[dict], verbose: bool) -> None:
508	    """Display diagnostic results."""
509	    table = Table(show_header=True, header_style="bold")
510	    table.add_column("Check", style="bold")
511	    table.add_column("Status", width=8)
512	    table.add_column("Value")
513	    if verbose:
514	        table.add_column("Notes", style="dim")
515	
516	    for check in checks:
517	        status = check["status"]
518	        if status == "ok":
519	            status_str = f"[green]{t('doctor_status_ok')}[/green]"
520	        elif status == "warning":
521	            status_str = f"[yellow]{t('doctor_status_warning')}[/yellow]"
522	        else:
523	            status_str = f"[red]{t('doctor_status_error')}[/red]"
524	
525	        if verbose:
526	            table.add_row(
527	                check["name"],
528	                status_str,
529	                check["value"],
530	                check.get("hint", ""),
531	            )
532	        else:
533	            table.add_row(
534	                check["name"],
535	                status_str,
536	                check["value"],
537	            )
538	
539	        # Show package details if verbose
540	        if verbose and "packages" in check:
541	            for pkg_name, pkg_version, pkg_status in check["packages"]:
542	                if pkg_status == "ok":
543	                    pkg_status_str = "[green]✓[/green]"
544	                elif pkg_status == "warning":
545	                    pkg_status_str = "[yellow]○[/yellow]"
546	                else:
547	                    pkg_status_str = "[red]✗[/red]"
548	
549	                table.add_row(
550	                    f"  └─ {pkg_name}",
551	                    pkg_status_str,
552	                    pkg_version,
553	                    "",
554	                )
555	
556	    console.print(table)
557

1	"""
2	Run command for kt-cli.
3	
4	Starts the model inference server using SGLang + kt-kernel.
5	"""
6	
7	import os
8	import subprocess
9	import sys
10	from pathlib import Path
11	from typing import Optional
12	
13	import click
14	import typer
15	
16	from kt_kernel.cli.config.settings import get_settings
17	from kt_kernel.cli.i18n import t
18	from kt_kernel.cli.utils.console import (
19	    confirm,
20	    console,
21	    print_api_info,
22	    print_error,
23	    print_info,
24	    print_server_info,
25	    print_step,
26	    print_success,
27	    print_warning,
28	    prompt_choice,
29	)
30	from kt_kernel.cli.utils.environment import detect_cpu_info, detect_gpus, detect_ram_gb
31	from kt_kernel.cli.utils.user_model_registry import UserModelRegistry
32	
33	
34	@click.command(
35	    context_settings={"ignore_unknown_options": True, "allow_extra_args": True},
36	    add_help_option=False,  # We'll handle help manually to avoid conflicts
37	)
38	@click.argument("model", required=False, default=None)
39	@click.option("--host", "-H", default=None, help="Server host address")
40	@click.option("--port", "-p", type=int, default=None, help="Server port")
41	@click.option("--gpu-experts", type=int, default=None, help="Number of GPU experts per layer")
42	@click.option("--cpu-threads", type=int, default=None, help="Number of CPU inference threads")
43	@click.option(
44	    "--numa-nodes",
45	    "numa_nodes",
46	    type=int,
47	    multiple=True,
48	    default=(),
49	    help="Number of KT threadpools, or explicit NUMA node IDs for each threadpool (e.g. --numa-nodes 2 or --numa-nodes 0 --numa-nodes 1)",
50	)
51	@click.option(
52	    "--tensor-parallel-size", "--tp", "tensor_parallel_size", type=int, default=None, help="Tensor parallel size"
53	)
54	@click.option("--model-path", type=click.Path(), default=None, help="Custom model path")
55	@click.option("--weights-path", type=click.Path(), default=None, help="Custom quantized weights path")
56	@click.option("--kt-method", default=None, help="KT quantization method")
57	@click.option(
58	    "--kt-gpu-prefill-threshold", "kt_gpu_prefill_threshold", type=int, default=None, help="GPU prefill token threshold"
59	)
60	@click.option("--attention-backend", default=None, help="Attention backend")
61	@click.option("--max-total-tokens", "max_total_tokens", type=int, default=None, help="Maximum total tokens")
62	@click.option("--max-running-requests", "max_running_requests", type=int, default=None, help="Maximum running requests")
63	@click.option("--chunked-prefill-size", "chunked_prefill_size", type=int, default=None, help="Chunked prefill size")
64	@click.option("--mem-fraction-static", "mem_fraction_static", type=float, default=None, help="Memory fraction static")
65	@click.option("--watchdog-timeout", "watchdog_timeout", type=int, default=None, help="Watchdog timeout")
66	@click.option("--served-model-name", "served_model_name", default=None, help="Served model name")
67	@click.option(
68	    "--disable-shared-experts-fusion",
69	    "disable_shared_experts_fusion",
70	    is_flag=True,
71	    default=None,
72	    help="Disable shared experts fusion",
73	)
74	@click.option(
75	    "--enable-shared-experts-fusion",
76	    "enable_shared_experts_fusion",
77	    is_flag=True,
78	    default=False,
79	    help="Enable shared experts fusion",
80	)
81	@click.option("--quantize", "-q", is_flag=True, default=False, help="Quantize model")
82	@click.option("--advanced", is_flag=True, default=False, help="Show advanced options")
83	@click.option("--dry-run", "dry_run", is_flag=True, default=False, help="Show command without executing")
84	@click.pass_context
85	def run(
86	    ctx: click.Context,
87	    model: Optional[str],
88	    host: Optional[str],
89	    port: Optional[int],
90	    gpu_experts: Optional[int],
91	    cpu_threads: Optional[int],
92	    numa_nodes: Optional[tuple[int, ...]],
93	    tensor_parallel_size: Optional[int],
94	    model_path: Optional[str],
95	    weights_path: Optional[str],
96	    kt_method: Optional[str],
97	    kt_gpu_prefill_threshold: Optional[int],
98	    attention_backend: Optional[str],
99	    max_total_tokens: Optional[int],
100	    max_running_requests: Optional[int],
101	    chunked_prefill_size: Optional[int],
102	    mem_fraction_static: Optional[float],
103	    watchdog_timeout: Optional[int],
104	    served_model_name: Optional[str],
105	    disable_shared_experts_fusion: Optional[bool],
106	    enable_shared_experts_fusion: bool,
107	    quantize: bool,
108	    advanced: bool,
109	    dry_run: bool,
110	) -> None:
111	    """Start model inference server.
112	
113	    \b
114	    Examples: kt run deepseek-v3 | kt run m2 --tensor-parallel-size 2 | kt run /path/to/model --gpu-experts 4
115	
116	    \b
117	    Custom Options: Pass any SGLang server option directly (e.g., kt run m2 --fp8-gemm-backend triton).
118	    Common: --fp8-gemm-backend, --tool-call-parser, --reasoning-parser, --dp-size, --enable-ma
119	    For full list: python -m sglang.launch_server --help
120	    """
121	    # Handle --help manually since we disabled it
122	    # Check sys.argv for --help or -h since ctx.args may not be set yet
123	    if "--help" in sys.argv or "-h" in sys.argv:
124	        click.echo(ctx.get_help())
125	        return
126	
127	    # Handle disable/enable shared experts fusion flags
128	    if enable_shared_experts_fusion:
129	        disable_shared_experts_fusion = False
130	
131	    # Convert Path objects from click
132	    model_path_obj = Path(model_path) if model_path else None
133	    weights_path_obj = Path(weights_path) if weights_path else None
134	
135	    # Get extra args that weren't parsed (unknown options)
136	    # click stores these in ctx.args when ignore_unknown_options=True
137	    extra_cli_args = list(ctx.args) if ctx.args else []
138	
139	    # Remove --help from extra args if present (already handled)
140	    extra_cli_args = [arg for arg in extra_cli_args if arg not in ["--help", "-h"]]
141	
142	    # Call the actual run function implementation
143	    _run_impl(
144	        model=model,
145	        host=host,
146	        port=port,
147	        gpu_experts=gpu_experts,
148	        cpu_threads=cpu_threads,
149	        numa_nodes=numa_nodes,
150	        tensor_parallel_size=tensor_parallel_size,
151	        model_path=model_path_obj,
152	        weights_path=weights_path_obj,
153	        kt_method=kt_method,
154	        kt_gpu_prefill_threshold=kt_gpu_prefill_threshold,
155	        attention_backend=attention_backend,
156	        max_total_tokens=max_total_tokens,
157	        max_running_requests=max_running_requests,
158	        chunked_prefill_size=chunked_prefill_size,
159	        mem_fraction_static=mem_fraction_static,
160	        watchdog_timeout=watchdog_timeout,
161	        served_model_name=served_model_name,
162	        disable_shared_experts_fusion=disable_shared_experts_fusion,
163	        quantize=quantize,
164	        advanced=advanced,
165	        dry_run=dry_run,
166	        extra_cli_args=extra_cli_args,
167	    )
168	
169	
170	def _run_impl(
171	    model: Optional[str],
172	    host: Optional[str],
173	    port: Optional[int],
174	    gpu_experts: Optional[int],
175	    cpu_threads: Optional[int],
176	    numa_nodes: Optional[tuple[int, ...]],
177	    tensor_parallel_size: Optional[int],
178	    model_path: Optional[Path],
179	    weights_path: Optional[Path],
180	    kt_method: Optional[str],
181	    kt_gpu_prefill_threshold: Optional[int],
182	    attention_backend: Optional[str],
183	    max_total_tokens: Optional[int],
184	    max_running_requests: Optional[int],
185	    chunked_prefill_size: Optional[int],
186	    mem_fraction_static: Optional[float],
187	    watchdog_timeout: Optional[int],
188	    served_model_name: Optional[str],
189	    disable_shared_experts_fusion: Optional[bool],
190	    quantize: bool,
191	    advanced: bool,
192	    dry_run: bool,
193	    extra_cli_args: list[str],
194	) -> None:
195	    """Actual implementation of run command."""
196	    # Check if SGLang is installed before proceeding
197	    from kt_kernel.cli.utils.sglang_checker import (
198	        check_sglang_installation,
199	        check_sglang_kt_kernel_support,
200	        print_sglang_install_instructions,
201	        print_sglang_kt_kernel_instructions,
202	    )
203	
204	    sglang_info = check_sglang_installation()
205	    if not sglang_info["installed"]:
206	        console.print()
207	        print_error(t("sglang_not_found"))
208	        console.print()
209	        print_sglang_install_instructions()
210	        raise typer.Exit(1)
211	
212	    # Check if SGLang supports kt-kernel (has --kt-gpu-prefill-token-threshold parameter)
213	    kt_kernel_support = check_sglang_kt_kernel_support()
214	    if not kt_kernel_support["supported"]:
215	        console.print()
216	        print_error(t("sglang_kt_kernel_not_supported"))
217	        console.print()
218	        print_sglang_kt_kernel_instructions()
219	        raise typer.Exit(1)
220	
221	    settings = get_settings()
222	    user_registry = UserModelRegistry()
223	
224	    # Check if we should use interactive mode
225	    # Interactive mode triggers when:
226	    # 1. No model specified, OR
227	    # 2. Model specified but missing critical parameters (gpu_experts, tensor_parallel_size, etc.)
228	    use_interactive = False
229	
230	    if model is None:
231	        use_interactive = True
232	    elif (
233	        gpu_experts is None
234	        or tensor_parallel_size is None

500	    # Prepare environment variables

509	    # Fail fast if a conflicting env var would crash sglang during model loading.
510	    # Check against the fully-assembled env dict (shell + kt config settings) so

512	    _check_conflicting_env_vars(final_kt_method, env)

514	    # Step 5: Show configuration summary

540	    console.print()

553	    # Execute with prepared environment variables

555	    # The actual startup takes time and these messages are misleading

583	# These functions were part of the old builtin model system

585	
586	def _check_conflicting_env_vars(kt_method: str, env: dict) -> None:
587	    """Exit early if environment variables conflict with the chosen kt-method.
588	
589	    Receives the fully-assembled subprocess env dict (shell + kt config settings)
590	    so that variables injected via inference.env or advanced.env are also caught.
591	    Catches copy-paste mistakes such as keeping SGLANG_DSV4_2604_SUBMODE=2604B
592	    in the shell after switching from a MXFP4 launch to another method.
593	    """
594	    dsv4_submode = env.get("SGLANG_DSV4_2604_SUBMODE", "")
595	    if dsv4_submode == "2604B" and (not kt_method or kt_method.upper() != "MXFP4"):
596	        print_error(
597	            f"SGLANG_DSV4_2604_SUBMODE=2604B is set but kt-method is "
598	            f"{kt_method!r} (not MXFP4). "
599	            f"This will raise a ValueError during model loading. "
600	            f"Either unset the variable (unset SGLANG_DSV4_2604_SUBMODE) "
601	            f"or switch to --kt-method MXFP4."
602	        )
603	        raise typer.Exit(1)
604	
605	
606	def _build_sglang_command(
607	    model_path: Path,
608	    weights_path: Optional[Path],
609	    host: str,
610	    port: int,
611	    gpu_experts: int,
612	    cpu_threads: int,
613	    numa_nodes: int,
614	    tensor_parallel_size: int,
615	    kt_method: str,
616	    kt_gpu_prefill_threshold: int,
617	    attention_backend: str,
618	    max_total_tokens: int,
619	    max_running_requests: int,
620	    chunked_prefill_size: int,
621	    mem_fraction_static: float,
622	    watchdog_timeout: int,
623	    served_model_name: str,
624	    disable_shared_experts_fusion: bool,
625	    kt_numa_nodes: Optional[list[int]],
626	    tool_call_parser: Optional[str],
627	    reasoning_parser: Optional[str],
628	    settings,
629	    extra_model_params: Optional[dict] = None,  # New parameter for additional params
630	    extra_cli_args: Optional[list[str]] = None,  # Extra args from CLI to pass to sglang
631	) -> list[str]:
632	    """Build the SGLang launch command."""
633	    cmd = [
634	        sys.executable,
635	        "-m",
636	        "sglang.launch_server",
637	        "--host",
638	        host,
639	        "--port",
640	        str(port),
641	        "--model",
642	        str(model_path),
643	    ]
644	
645	    # Add kt-kernel options
646	    # kt-kernel is needed for:
647	    # 1. Quantized models (when weights_path is provided)
648	    # 2. MoE models with CPU offloading (when kt-cpuinfer > 0 or kt-num-gpu-experts is configured)
649	    use_kt_kernel = False
650	
651	    # Check if we should use kt-kernel
652	    if weights_path:
653	        # Quantized model - always use kt-kernel
654	        use_kt_kernel = True
655	    elif cpu_threads > 0 or gpu_experts > 1:
656	        # CPU offloading configured - use kt-kernel
657	        use_kt_kernel = True
658	
659	    if use_kt_kernel:
660	        # Add kt-weight-path: use quantized weights if available, otherwise use model path
661	        weight_path_to_use = weights_path if weights_path else model_path
662	
663	        # Add kt-kernel configuration
664	        cmd.extend(
665	            [
666	                "--kt-weight-path",
667	                str(weight_path_to_use),
668	                "--kt-cpuinfer",
669	                str(cpu_threads),
670	                "--kt-threadpool-count",
671	                str(numa_nodes),
672	                "--kt-num-gpu-experts",
673	                str(gpu_experts),
674	                "--kt-method",
675	                kt_method,
676	                "--kt-gpu-prefill-token-threshold",
677	                str(kt_gpu_prefill_threshold),
678	                "--kt-enable-dynamic-expert-update",  # Enable dynamic expert updates
679	            ]
680	        )
681	        if kt_numa_nodes is not None:
682	            cmd.extend(["--kt-numa-nodes", *map(str, kt_numa_nodes)])
683	
684	    # Add SGLang options
685	    cmd.extend(
686	        [
687	            "--attention-backend",
688	            attention_backend,
689	            "--trust-remote-code",
690	            "--mem-fraction-static",
691	            str(mem_fraction_static),
692	            "--chunked-prefill-size",
693	            str(chunked_prefill_size),
694	            "--max-running-requests",
695	            str(max_running_requests),
696	            "--max-total-tokens",
697	            str(max_total_tokens),
698	            "--watchdog-timeout",
699	            str(watchdog_timeout),
700	            "--enable-mixed-chunk",
701	            "--tensor-parallel-size",
702	            str(tensor_parallel_size),
703	            "--enable-p2p-check",
704	        ]
705	    )
706	
707	    # Add served model name if specified
708	    if served_model_name:
709	        cmd.extend(["--served-model-name", served_model_name])
710	
711	    # Add performance flags
712	    if disable_shared_experts_fusion:
713	        cmd.append("--disable-shared-experts-fusion")
714	
715	    # Add FP8 backend if using FP8 method
716	    if "FP8" in kt_method.upper():
717	        cmd.extend(["--fp8-gemm-backend", "triton"])
718	
719	    # Add parsers if specified
720	    if tool_call_parser:
721	        cmd.extend(["--tool-call-parser", tool_call_parser])
722	    if reasoning_parser:
723	        cmd.extend(["--reasoning-parser", reasoning_parser])
724	
725	    # Add any extra parameters from model defaults that weren't explicitly handled
726	    if extra_model_params:
727	        # List of parameters already handled above
728	        handled_params = {
729	            "kt-num-gpu-experts",
730	            "kt-cpuinfer",
731	            "kt-threadpool-count",
732	            "kt-numa-nodes",
733	            "kt-method",
734	            "kt-gpu-prefill-token-threshold",
735	            "attention-backend",
736	            "tensor-parallel-size",
737	            "max-total-tokens",
738	            "max-running-requests",
739	            "chunked-prefill-size",
740	            "mem-fraction-static",
741	            "watchdog-timeout",
742	            "served-model-name",
743	            "disable-shared-experts-fusion",
744	        }
745	
746	        for key, value in extra_model_params.items():
747	            if key not in handled_params:
748	                # Add unhandled parameters dynamically
749	                cmd.append(f"--{key}")
750	                if isinstance(value, bool):
751	                    # Boolean flags don't need a value
752	                    if not value:
753	                        # For False boolean, skip the flag entirely
754	                        cmd.pop()  # Remove the flag we just added
755	                else:
756	                    cmd.append(str(value))
757	
758	    # Add extra args from settings
759	    extra_args = settings.get("advanced.sglang_args", [])
760	    if extra_args:
761	        cmd.extend(extra_args)
762	
763	    # Add extra CLI args (user-provided options not defined in kt CLI)
764	    if extra_cli_args:
765	        cmd.extend(extra_cli_args)
766	
767	    return cmd
768

771	    """Show interactive model selection interface.
772	
773	    Returns:
774	        Selected model name or None if cancelled.
775	    """
776	    from rich.panel import Panel

782	    if not all_models:
783	        console.print()
784	        print_warning("No models registered.")
785	        console.print()
786	        console.print(f"  Add models with: [cyan]kt model scan[/cyan]")
787	        console.print(f"  Or manually: [cyan]kt model add /path/to/model[/cyan]")
788	        console.print()
789	        return None
790	
791	    console.print()
792	    console.print(
793	        Panel.fit(
794	            "Select a model to run",
795	            border_style="cyan",
796	        )
797	    )
798	    console.print()
799	
800	    # Build choices list
801	    choices = []
802	    choice_map = {}  # index -> model name

805	    console.print(f"[bold green]Available Models:[/bold green]")

808	    for i, model in enumerate(all_models, 1):
809	        # Check if path exists
810	        path_status = "✓" if model.path_exists() else "✗ Missing"
811	        console.print(f"  [cyan][{i}][/cyan] [bold]{model.name}[/bold] [{path_status}]")
812	        console.print(f"      [dim]{model.format} - {model.path}[/dim]")
813	        choices.append(str(i))
814	        choice_map[str(i)] = model.name
815	
816	    console.print()
817	
818	    # Add cancel option
819	    cancel_idx = str(len(choices) + 1)
820	    console.print(f"  [cyan][{cancel_idx}][/cyan] [dim]Cancel[/dim]")
821	    choices.append(cancel_idx)
822	    console.print()
823	
824	    # Prompt for selection
825	    try:
826	        selection = Prompt.ask(
827	            "Select model",
828	            choices=choices,
829	            default="1" if choices else cancel_idx,
830	        )
831	    except KeyboardInterrupt:
832	        console.print()
833	        return None
834	
835	    if selection == cancel_idx:
836	        return None
837	
838	    return choice_map.get(selection)

27	# Common SHA256 status display mapping used across multiple commands
28	SHA256_STATUS_MAP = {
29	    "not_checked": "[dim]Not Checked[/dim]",
30	    "checking": "[yellow]Checking...[/yellow]",
31	    "passed": "[green]✓ Passed[/green]",
32	    "failed": "[red]✗ Failed[/red]",
33	    "no_repo": "[dim]-[/dim]",
34	}

36	# Plain text version for panels and verbose output
37	SHA256_STATUS_MAP_PLAIN = {
38	    "not_checked": "Not Checked",

46	def is_amx_weights(model_path) -> tuple[bool, int]:
47	    """
48	    Determine if a model uses AMX weights and count NUMA nodes.
49	
50	    Returns:
51	        (is_amx, numa_count): Tuple where is_amx indicates AMX weights,
52	        and numa_count is the number of NUMA nodes (0 if not AMX).
53	    """
54	    import re
55	    from pathlib import Path
56	    from safetensors import safe_open
57	
58	    model_path = Path(model_path)
59	    safetensors_files = sorted(model_path.glob("*.safetensors"))
60	
61	    if not safetensors_files:
62	        return False, 0
63	
64	    numa_indices = set()
65	    numa_pattern = re.compile(r"\.numa\.(\d+)\.")
66	
67	    # Check first 3 files for NUMA keys
68	    for file_path in safetensors_files[:3]:
69	        try:
70	            with safe_open(file_path, framework="pt", device="cpu") as f:
71	                for key in f.keys():
72	                    if ".numa." in key:
73	                        match = numa_pattern.search(key)
74	                        if match:
75	                            numa_indices.add(int(match.group(1)))
76	        except Exception:
77	            continue
78	
79	    if not numa_indices:
80	        return False, 0
81	
82	    return True, len(numa_indices)
83

492	        except (ImportError, Exception):

496	                from analyze_moe_model import analyze_moe_model
497	            except (ImportError, Exception):

510	        console.print()

512	
513	    # Check for models with non-existent paths and remove them automatically

519	    if models_to_remove:

532	            console.print()
533	            console.print(f"  {t('model_scan_hint')} [cyan]kt model scan[/cyan]")
534	            console.print(f"  {t('model_add_hint')} [cyan]kt model add <path>[/cyan]")
535	            console.print()
536	            return

538	    if verbose:
539	        # Verbose mode: detailed cards
540	        console.print(f"[bold cyan]{t('model_registered_models_title')}[/bold cyan]\n")
541	
542	        for i, model in enumerate(models, 1):
543	            # Check if path exists

556	            # Calculate folder size if exists

611	        # Pre-analyze GPU MoE models concurrently if enabled
612	        moe_results = {}
613	        moe_failed_models = []  # Track models that failed MoE analysis
614	        if show_moe and analyze_moe_model and gpu_models:
615	            from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed
616	            import threading
617	
618	            # Collect GPU models that need MoE analysis
619	            # Priority: use cached MoE info from UserModel, only analyze if is_moe is None
620	            models_to_analyze = []

622	
623	            for model in gpu_models:
624	                # Check if MoE info is already cached in UserModel (and not using --no-cache)
625	                if not no_cache and model.is_moe is not None:
626	                    # Use cached info from UserModel
627	                    if model.is_moe:
628	                        moe_results[model.name] = {
629	                            "is_moe": True,
630	                            "num_experts": model.moe_num_experts,

632	                            "cached": True,

635	                else:

637	                    path_obj = Path(model.path)

639	                    models_need_update.append(model)
640	
641	            if models_to_analyze:
642	                # Use lock for thread-safe console output
643	                print_lock = threading.Lock()
644	                completed_count = [0]  # Use list to allow modification in nested function
645	
646	                def analyze_with_progress(model_info):
647	                    model_name, model_path = model_info
648	                    try:
649	                        with print_lock:
650	                            console.print(f"[dim]Analyzing MoE: {model_name}...[/dim]")
651	                        result = analyze_moe_model(model_path, use_cache=not no_cache)
652	
653	                        # Check if analysis returned valid results
654	                        if result is None or result.get("num_experts", 0) == 0:
655	                            with print_lock:
656	                                completed_count[0] += 1

658	                                    f"[dim]✗ [{completed_count[0]}/{len(models_to_analyze)}] {model_name} - Not a MoE model or analysis failed[/dim]"
659	                                )
660	                            return (model_name, None, "Not a MoE model or analysis failed")
661	
662	                        with print_lock:
663	                            completed_count[0] += 1
664	                            cached_tag = "[green](cached)[/green]" if result and result.get("cached") else ""
665	                            console.print(
666	                                f"[dim]✓ [{completed_count[0]}/{len(models_to_analyze)}] {model_name} {cached_tag}[/dim]"
667	                            )
668	                        return (model_name, result, None)
669	                    except Exception as e:
670	                        with print_lock:
671	                            completed_count[0] += 1
672	                            error_msg = str(e)[:80]
673	                            console.print(
674	                                f"[dim]✗ [{completed_count[0]}/{len(models_to_analyze)}] {model_name} - Error: {error_msg}[/dim]"
675	                            )
676	                        return (model_name, None, error_msg)
677	
678	                if no_cache:

680	                console.print(
681	                    f"\n[cyan]Analyzing {len(models_to_analyze)} MoE model(s) with {min(16, len(models_to_analyze))} threads...[/cyan]\n"

683	
684	                # Analyze concurrently with up to 16 workers

1018	@app.command(name="clear-cache")
1019	def clear_cache() -> None:
1020	    """Clear MoE analysis cache."""
1021	    from pathlib import Path
1022	    import json
1023	
1024	    cache_file = Path.home() / ".ktransformers" / "cache" / "moe_analysis.json"
1025	
1026	    if not cache_file.exists():
1027	        console.print()
1028	        console.print("[dim]No MoE cache found.[/dim]")
1029	        console.print()
1030	        return
1031	
1032	    # Read cache to count entries
1033	    try:
1034	        with open(cache_file, "r") as f:
1035	            cache_data = json.load(f)
1036	        cache_count = len(cache_data)
1037	    except Exception:
1038	        cache_count = 0
1039	
1040	    if cache_count == 0:
1041	        console.print()
1042	        console.print("[dim]MoE cache is empty.[/dim]")
1043	        console.print()
1044	        return
1045	
1046	    console.print()
1047	    console.print(f"[yellow]Found {cache_count} cached model(s) in:[/yellow]")
1048	    console.print(f"  {cache_file}")
1049	    console.print()
1050	
1051	    if confirm("Clear all MoE analysis cache?", default=False):
1052	        cache_file.unlink()
1053	        console.print(f"[green]✓ Cleared cache for {cache_count} model(s)[/green]")
1054	    else:
1055	        console.print("[dim]Cache clear cancelled.[/dim]")
1056	
1057	    console.print()

1076	@app.command(name="link-cpu")
1077	def link_cpu(
1078	    cpu_model: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Name of the CPU model (GGUF/AMX)"),
1079	    gpu_models: List[str] = typer.Argument(..., help="Name(s) of GPU model(s) to link with"),
1080	) -> None:
1081	    """Link a CPU model (GGUF/AMX) with one or more GPU models for joint startup."""
1082	    from kt_kernel.cli.utils.user_model_registry import UserModelRegistry
1083	
1084	    registry = UserModelRegistry()
1085	
1086	    # Check if CPU model exists
1087	    cpu_model_obj = registry.get_model(cpu_model)
1088	    if not cpu_model_obj:

1090	        console.print()
1091	        console.print(f"  Use [cyan]kt model list[/cyan] to see registered models")
1092	        console.print()
1093	        raise typer.Exit(1)
1094	
1095	    # Check if it's actually a CPU model (GGUF or AMX)
1096	    if cpu_model_obj.format == "safetensors":
1097	        # Check if it's AMX by looking for .numa. pattern

1104	            raise typer.Exit(1)

1106	    # Verify all GPU models exist and collect their UUIDs
1107	    gpu_model_uuids = []

1109	    for gpu_name in gpu_models:

1120	        console.print()
1121	        raise typer.Exit(1)
1122	
1123	    # Update the CPU model with GPU links (using UUIDs for stability)
1124	    registry.update_model(cpu_model, {"gpu_model_ids": gpu_model_uuids})
1125	
1126	    console.print()
1127	    print_success(f"Linked CPU model '{cpu_model}' with GPU model(s):")
1128	    for gpu_name in gpu_models:
1129	        console.print(f"  [green]✓[/green] {gpu_name}")
1130	    console.print()
1131	    console.print(f"  View the relationship with [cyan]kt model list[/cyan]")
1132	    console.print()

1300	                    )

1919	@app.command(name="remove")
1920	def remove_model(
1921	    name: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Name of model to remove"),
1922	    yes: bool = typer.Option(False, "--yes", "-y", help="Skip confirmation"),
1923	) -> None:
1924	    """Remove a model from the registry (does not delete files)."""
1925	    from kt_kernel.cli.utils.user_model_registry import UserModelRegistry
1926	
1927	    registry = UserModelRegistry()
1928	
1929	    # Check if model exists
1930	    model = registry.get_model(name)
1931	    if not model:
1932	        print_error(t("model_remove_not_found", name=name))
1933	        console.print()
1934	        console.print(f"  {t('model_remove_list_hint')} [cyan]kt model list[/cyan]")
1935	        console.print()
1936	        raise typer.Exit(1)
1937	
1938	    console.print()
1939	    console.print(f"[bold yellow]{t('model_remove_warning')}[/bold yellow]")
1940	    console.print(f"  {t('model_remove_note')}")
1941	    console.print(f"  [dim]Path: {model.path}[/dim]")
1942	    console.print()
1943	
1944	    # Check if this GPU model is linked by any CPU models
1945	    model_uuid = model.id
1946	    affected_cpu_models = []
1947	
1948	    # Only check for GPU models (safetensors that are not AMX)
1949	    if model.format == "safetensors":
1950	        is_amx, _ = is_amx_weights(model.path)
1951	        if not is_amx:
1952	            # This is a GPU model, check for CPU models that link to it
1953	            for m in registry.list_models():
1954	                if m.gpu_model_ids and model_uuid in m.gpu_model_ids:
1955	                    affected_cpu_models.append(m)
1956	
1957	    # If there are affected CPU models, inform the user
1958	    if affected_cpu_models:
1959	        console.print(f"[yellow]This GPU model is linked by {len(affected_cpu_models)} CPU model(s):[/yellow]")
1960	        for cpu_model in affected_cpu_models:
1961	            console.print(f"  • {cpu_model.name}")
1962	        console.print()
1963	        console.print(f"[dim]These links will be automatically removed.[/dim]")
1964	        console.print()
1965	
1966	    # Confirm deletion
1967	    if not yes:
1968	        if not confirm(t("model_remove_confirm", name=name), default=False):
1969	            print_info(t("model_remove_cancelled"))
1970	            console.print()
1971	            return
1972	
1973	    # Clean up references in CPU models before removing
1974	    if affected_cpu_models:
1975	        for cpu_model in affected_cpu_models:
1976	            # Remove this GPU model's UUID from the cpu_model's gpu_model_ids list
1977	            new_gpu_ids = [gid for gid in cpu_model.gpu_model_ids if gid != model_uuid]
1978	            registry.update_model(cpu_model.name, {"gpu_model_ids": new_gpu_ids if new_gpu_ids else None})
1979	
1980	    # Remove from registry
1981	    if registry.remove_model(name):
1982	        console.print()
1983	        print_success(t("model_removed", name=name))
1984	        console.print()
1985	    else:
1986	        print_error(t("model_remove_failed", name=name))
1987	        raise typer.Exit(1)

1993	    from rich.table import Table

2091	    name: str = typer.Argument(None, help="Name of model to verify (interactive if not provided)"),
2092	    verbose: bool = typer.Option(False, "--verbose", "-v", help="Show detailed SHA256 comparison for each file"),
2093	) -> None:
2094	    """Verify model integrity using SHA256 checksums with interactive repair."""
2095	    from pathlib import Path
2096	    from rich.prompt import Prompt, Confirm
2097	    from rich.table import Table
2098	    from rich.progress import Progress, SpinnerColumn, BarColumn, TextColumn, TimeElapsedColumn, MofNCompleteColumn
2099	    from kt_kernel.cli.utils.user_model_registry import UserModelRegistry
2100	    from kt_kernel.cli.utils.model_verifier import verify_model_integrity_with_progress, check_huggingface_connectivity
2101	
2102	    registry = UserModelRegistry()
2103	
2104	    # Helper function to display model selection table
2105	    def show_model_table():
2106	        from kt_kernel.cli.utils.model_scanner import format_size
2107	        from pathlib import Path
2108	
2109	        # Import MoE analyzer
2110	        analyze_moe_model = None
2111	        try:
2112	            from kt_kernel.cli.utils.analyze_moe_model import analyze_moe_model
2113	        except ImportError:
2114	            pass
2115	
2116	        all_models = registry.list_models()
2117	
2118	        # Filter: only safetensors models with repo_id
2119	        verifiable_models = [m for m in all_models if m.repo_id and m.format == "safetensors"]
2120	
2121	        if not verifiable_models:
2122	            print_warning(t("model_verify_all_no_repos"))
2123	            console.print()
2124	            console.print(f"  {t('model_verify_all_config_hint')}")
2125	            console.print()
2126	            return None
2127	
2128	        # Analyze MoE models
2129	        moe_results = {}

2662	        print_warning(t("model_verify_all_no_repos"))
2663	        console.print()
2664	        console.print(f"  {t('model_verify_all_config_hint')} [cyan]kt model edit <name>[/cyan]")
2665	        console.print()
2666	        return
2667	
2668	    console.print()
2669	    print_warning(t("model_verify_not_implemented"))
2670	    console.print()
2671	    console.print(f"  {t('model_verify_all_found', count=len(models_with_repo))}")
2672	    console.print()
2673	
2674	    for model in models_with_repo:
2675	        console.print(f"  • {model.name} ({model.repo_type}:{model.repo_id})")
2676	
2677	    console.print()
2678	    console.print(f"  [dim]{t('model_verify_future_note')}[/dim]")
2679	    console.print()
2680	    console.print(f"  {t('model_verify_all_manual_hint')} [cyan]kt model verify <name>[/cyan]")
2681	    console.print()
2682	
2683	
2684	@app.command(name="auto-repo")
2685	def auto_detect_repo(
2686	    apply: bool = typer.Option(
2687	        False, "--apply", "-a", help="Automatically apply detected repo information without confirmation"
2688	    ),
2689	    dry_run: bool = typer.Option(
2690	        False, "--dry-run", "-d", help="Show what would be detected without making any changes"
2691	    ),
2692	) -> None:
2693	    """
2694	    Auto-detect repository information from model README.md files.
2695	
2696	    Scans all models without repo_id (safetensors/gguf only) and attempts to
2697	    extract repository information from README.md metadata (license_link field).
2698	
2699	    Examples:
2700	        kt model auto-repo              # Scan and ask for confirmation
2701	        kt model auto-repo --apply      # Scan and apply automatically
2702	        kt model auto-repo --dry-run    # Scan only, no changes
2703	    """
2704	    from kt_kernel.cli.utils.user_model_registry import UserModelRegistry
2705	    from kt_kernel.cli.utils.repo_detector import scan_models_for_repo, format_detection_report, apply_detection_results
2706	    from rich.table import Table
2707	
2708	    console.print()
2709	    print_info("Scanning models for repository information...")
2710	    console.print()
2711	
2712	    # Get all models
2713	    registry = UserModelRegistry()
2714	    models = registry.list_models()
2715	
2716	    if not models:
2717	        print_warning("No models found in registry")
2718	        console.print()
2719	        return
2720	
2721	    # Scan for repo information
2722	    print_step("Analyzing README.md files...")
2723	    results = scan_models_for_repo(models)
2724	
2725	    # Show results
2726	    console.print()
2727	
2728	    if not results["detected"] and not results["not_detected"]:
2729	        print_info("All models already have repository information configured")
2730	        console.print()
2731	        return
2732	
2733	    # Create results table
2734	    if results["detected"]:
2735	        console.print("[bold green]✓ Detected Repository Information[/bold green]")
2736	        console.print()
2737	
2738	        table = Table(show_header=True, header_style="bold cyan")
2739	        table.add_column("Model Name", style="yellow")
2740	        table.add_column("Repository", style="cyan")
2741	        table.add_column("Type", style="magenta")
2742	
2743	        for model, repo_id, repo_type in results["detected"]:
2744	            table.add_row(model.name, repo_id, repo_type)
2745	
2746	        console.print(table)
2747	        console.print()
2748	
2749	    if results["not_detected"]:
2750	        console.print(
2751	            f"[bold yellow]✗ No Repository Information Found ({len(results['not_detected'])} models)[/bold yellow]"
2752	        )
2753	        console.print()
2754	
2755	        for model in results["not_detected"][:5]:  # Show first 5
2756	            console.print(f"  • {model.name}")
2757	
2758	        if len(results["not_detected"]) > 5:
2759	            console.print(f"  ... and {len(results['not_detected']) - 5} more")
2760	
2761	        console.print()
2762	
2763	    if results["skipped"]:
2764	        console.print(
2765	            f"[dim]⊘ Skipped {len(results['skipped'])} models (already configured or not safetensors/gguf)[/dim]"
2766	        )
2767	        console.print()
2768	
2769	    # Summary
2770	    console.print("[bold]Summary:[/bold]")
2771	    console.print(f"  • [green]{len(results['detected'])}[/green] detected")
2772	    console.print(f"  • [yellow]{len(results['not_detected'])}[/yellow] not detected")
2773	    console.print(f"  • [dim]{len(results['skipped'])}[/dim] skipped")
2774	    console.print()
2775	
2776	    # Exit if dry run or no detections
2777	    if dry_run:
2778	        print_info("Dry run mode - no changes made")
2779	        console.print()
2780	        return
2781	
2782	    if not results["detected"]:
2783	        console.print()
2784	        return
2785	
2786	    # Ask for confirmation (unless --apply flag)
2787	    if not apply:
2788	        console.print()
2789	        if not confirm(f"Apply repository information to {len(results['detected'])} model(s)?", default=False):
2790	            print_warning("Cancelled - no changes made")
2791	            console.print()
2792	            return
2793	
2794	    # Apply changes
2795	    console.print()
2796	    print_step("Applying changes...")
2797	
2798	    updated_count = apply_detection_results(results, registry)
2799	
2800	    console.print()
2801	    if updated_count > 0:
2802	        print_success(f"✓ Updated {updated_count} model(s) with repository information")
2803	        console.print()
2804	        console.print("  You can now:")
2805	        console.print("  • Run [cyan]kt model verify <name>[/cyan] to verify model integrity")
2806	        console.print("  • Check status with [cyan]kt model list[/cyan]")
2807	        console.print()
2808	    else:
2809	        print_error("Failed to update models")
2810	        console.print()
2811
