1	"""
2	Config command for kt-cli.
3	
4	Manages kt-cli configuration.
5	"""
6	
7	from typing import Optional
8	
9	import typer
10	import yaml
11	from rich.syntax import Syntax
12	
13	from kt_kernel.cli.config.settings import get_settings
14	from kt_kernel.cli.i18n import t
15	from kt_kernel.cli.utils.console import confirm, console, print_error, print_success
16	
17	app = typer.Typer(help="Manage kt-cli configuration")
18	
19	
20	@app.command(name="init")
21	def init() -> None:
22	    """Initialize or re-run the first-time setup wizard."""
23	    from kt_kernel.cli.main import _show_first_run_setup
24	    from kt_kernel.cli.config.settings import get_settings
25	
26	    settings = get_settings()
27	    _show_first_run_setup(settings)
28	
29	
30	@app.command(name="show")
31	def show(
32	    key: Optional[str] = typer.Argument(None, help="Configuration key to show (e.g., server.port)"),
33	) -> None:
34	    """Show current configuration."""
35	    settings = get_settings()
36	
37	    if key:
38	        value = settings.get(key)
39	        if value is not None:
40	            if isinstance(value, (dict, list)):
41	                console.print(yaml.dump({key: value}, default_flow_style=False, allow_unicode=True))
42	            else:
43	                console.print(t("config_get_value", key=key, value=value))
44	        else:
45	            print_error(t("config_get_not_found", key=key))
46	            raise typer.Exit(1)
47	    else:
48	        console.print(f"\n[bold]{t('config_show_title')}[/bold]\n")
49	        console.print(f"[dim]{t('config_file_location', path=str(settings.config_path))}[/dim]\n")
50	
51	        config_yaml = yaml.dump(settings.get_all(), default_flow_style=False, allow_unicode=True)
52	        syntax = Syntax(config_yaml, "yaml", theme="monokai", line_numbers=False)
53	        console.print(syntax)
54	
55	
56	@app.command(name="set")
57	def set_config(
58	    key: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Configuration key (e.g., server.port)"),
59	    value: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Value to set"),
60	) -> None:
61	    """Set a configuration value."""
62	    settings = get_settings()
63	
64	    # Try to parse value as JSON/YAML for complex types
65	    parsed_value = _parse_value(value)
66	
67	    settings.set(key, parsed_value)
68	    print_success(t("config_set_success", key=key, value=parsed_value))
69	
70	
71	@app.command(name="get")
72	def get_config(
73	    key: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Configuration key (e.g., server.port)"),
74	) -> None:
75	    """Get a configuration value."""
76	    settings = get_settings()
77	    value = settings.get(key)
78	
79	    if value is not None:
80	        if isinstance(value, (dict, list)):
81	            console.print(yaml.dump(value, default_flow_style=False, allow_unicode=True))
82	        else:
83	            console.print(str(value))
84	    else:
85	        print_error(t("config_get_not_found", key=key))
86	        raise typer.Exit(1)
87	
88	
89	@app.command(name="reset")
90	def reset(
91	    yes: bool = typer.Option(False, "--yes", "-y", help="Skip confirmation"),
92	) -> None:
93	    """Reset configuration to defaults."""
94	    if not yes:
95	        if not confirm(t("config_reset_confirm"), default=False):
96	            raise typer.Abort()
97	
98	    settings = get_settings()
99	    settings.reset()
100	    print_success(t("config_reset_success"))
101	
102	
103	@app.command(name="path")
104	def path() -> None:
105	    """Show configuration file path."""
106	    settings = get_settings()
107	    console.print(str(settings.config_path))
108	
109	
110	@app.command(name="model-path-list", deprecated=True, hidden=True)
111	def model_path_list() -> None:
112	    """[Deprecated] Use 'kt model path-list' instead."""
113	    console.print("[yellow]⚠ This command is deprecated. Use 'kt model path-list' instead.[/yellow]\n")
114	    import subprocess
115	    subprocess.run(["kt", "model", "path-list"])
116	
117	
118	@app.command(name="model-path-add", deprecated=True, hidden=True)
119	def model_path_add(
120	    path: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Path to add"),
121	) -> None:
122	    """[Deprecated] Use 'kt model path-add' instead."""
123	    console.print("[yellow]⚠ This command is deprecated. Use 'kt model path-add' instead.[/yellow]\n")
124	    import subprocess
125	    subprocess.run(["kt", "model", "path-add", path])
126	
127	
128	@app.command(name="model-path-remove", deprecated=True, hidden=True)
129	def model_path_remove(
130	    path: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Path to remove"),
131	) -> None:
132	    """[Deprecated] Use 'kt model path-remove' instead."""
133	    console.print("[yellow]⚠ This command is deprecated. Use 'kt model path-remove' instead.[/yellow]\n")
134	    import subprocess
135	    subprocess.run(["kt", "model", "path-remove", path])
136	
137	
138	def _parse_value(value: str):
139	    """Parse a string value into appropriate Python type."""
140	    # Try boolean
141	    if value.lower() in ("true", "yes", "on", "1"):
142	        return True
143	    if value.lower() in ("false", "no", "off", "0"):
144	        return False
145	
146	    # Try integer
147	    try:
148	        return int(value)
149	    except ValueError:
150	        pass
151	
152	    # Try float
153	    try:
154	        return float(value)
155	    except ValueError:
156	        pass
157	
158	    # Try YAML/JSON parsing for lists/dicts
159	    try:
160	        parsed = yaml.safe_load(value)
161	        if isinstance(parsed, (dict, list)):
162	            return parsed
163	    except yaml.YAMLError:
164	        pass
165	
166	    # Return as string
167	    return value
168

1	"""
2	Quant command for kt-cli.
3	
4	Quantizes model weights for CPU inference.
5	"""
6	
7	import subprocess
8	import sys
9	from enum import Enum
10	from pathlib import Path
11	from typing import Optional
12	
13	import typer
14	
15	from kt_kernel.cli.config.settings import get_settings
16	from kt_kernel.cli.i18n import t
17	from kt_kernel.cli.utils.console import (
18	    confirm,
19	    console,
20	    create_progress,
21	    print_error,
22	    print_info,
23	    print_step,
24	    print_success,
25	    print_warning,
26	)
27	from kt_kernel.cli.utils.environment import detect_cpu_info
28	
29	
30	class QuantMethod(str, Enum):
31	    """Quantization method."""
32	
33	    INT4 = "int4"
34	    INT8 = "int8"
35	
36	
37	def quant(
38	    model: Optional[str] = typer.Argument(
39	        None,
40	        help="Model name or path to quantize",
41	    ),
42	    method: Optional[QuantMethod] = typer.Option(
43	        None,
44	        "--method",
45	        "-m",
46	        help="Quantization method",
47	    ),
48	    output: Optional[Path] = typer.Option(
49	        None,
50	        "--output",
51	        "-o",
52	        help="Output path for quantized weights",
53	    ),
54	    input_type: Optional[str] = typer.Option(
55	        None,
56	        "--input-type",
57	        "-i",
58	        help="Input weight type (fp8, fp16, bf16)",
59	    ),
60	    cpu_threads: Optional[int] = typer.Option(
61	        None,
62	        "--cpu-threads",
63	        help="Number of CPU threads for quantization",
64	    ),
65	    numa_nodes: Optional[int] = typer.Option(
66	        None,
67	        "--numa-nodes",
68	        help="Number of NUMA nodes",
69	    ),
70	    no_merge: bool = typer.Option(
71	        False,
72	        "--no-merge",
73	        help="Don't merge safetensor files",
74	    ),
75	    gpu: bool = typer.Option(
76	        False,
77	        "--gpu",
78	        help="Use GPU for conversion (faster)",
79	    ),
80	    yes: bool = typer.Option(
81	        False,
82	        "--yes",
83	        "-y",
84	        help="Skip confirmation prompts",
85	    ),
86	) -> None:
87	    """Quantize model weights for CPU inference.
88	
89	    If no model is specified, interactive mode will be activated.
90	    """
91	    settings = get_settings()
92	
93	    # Check if we should use interactive mode
94	    # Interactive mode triggers when: no model, or missing critical parameters
95	    needs_interactive = model is None or method is None or cpu_threads is None or numa_nodes is None
96	    is_interactive = False
97	
98	    if needs_interactive and sys.stdin.isatty():
99	        # Use interactive configuration (includes verification in Step 1.5)
100	        from kt_kernel.cli.utils.quant_interactive import interactive_quant_config
101	
102	        console.print()
103	        console.print(f"[bold cyan]═══ {t('quant_interactive_title')} ═══[/bold cyan]")
104	        console.print()
105	        console.print(f"[yellow]{t('quant_new_model_notice')}[/yellow]")
106	        console.print()
107	
108	        config = interactive_quant_config()
109	        if config is None:
110	            # User cancelled
111	            raise typer.Exit(0)
112	
113	        # Extract configuration
114	        model_obj = config["model"]
115	        model = model_obj.id
116	        input_path = Path(model_obj.path)
117	        method = QuantMethod(config["method"])
118	        input_type = config["input_type"]
119	        cpu_threads = config["cpu_threads"]
120	        numa_nodes = config["numa_nodes"]
121	        output = config["output_path"]
122	        gpu = config["use_gpu"]
123	        is_interactive = True
124	
125	        console.print()
126	        print_success(t("quant_config_complete"))
127	        console.print()
128	    else:
129	        # Non-interactive mode - require model parameter
130	        if model is None:
131	            print_error("Model argument is required in non-interactive mode")
132	            console.print()
133	            console.print("Usage: kt quant <model>")
134	            console.print("   Or: kt quant  (for interactive mode)")
135	            raise typer.Exit(1)
136	
137	        # Set defaults for optional parameters
138	        method = method or QuantMethod.INT4
139	        input_type = input_type or "fp8"
140	
141	        console.print()
142	
143	        # Resolve input path
144	        input_path = _resolve_input_path(model, settings)
145	        if input_path is None:
146	            print_error(t("quant_input_not_found", path=model))
147	            raise typer.Exit(1)
148	
149	        # Pre-quantization verification (only in non-interactive mode)
150	        # Interactive mode already did verification in interactive_quant_config()
151	        from kt_kernel.cli.utils.user_model_registry import UserModelRegistry
152	        from kt_kernel.cli.utils.model_verifier import pre_operation_verification
153	
154	        user_registry = UserModelRegistry()
155	        user_model_obj = user_registry.find_by_path(str(input_path))
156	
157	        if user_model_obj and user_model_obj.format == "safetensors":
158	            pre_operation_verification(user_model_obj, user_registry, operation_name="quantizing")
159	
160	    # Get user model info for both modes (needed later for registering quantized model)
161	    from kt_kernel.cli.utils.user_model_registry import UserModelRegistry
162	
163	    user_registry = UserModelRegistry()
164	    user_model_obj = user_registry.find_by_path(str(input_path))
165	
166	    # Validate that it's a MoE model (not AMX or GGUF)
167	    from kt_kernel.cli.commands.model import is_amx_weights
168	
169	    # Check if it's AMX (already quantized)
170	    is_amx, _ = is_amx_weights(str(input_path))
171	    if is_amx:
172	        print_error("Cannot quantize AMX models (already quantized)")
173	        console.print()
174	        console.print(f"  The model at {input_path} is already in AMX format.")
175	        raise typer.Exit(1)
176	
177	    # Check if it's a MoE model
178	    from kt_kernel.cli.utils.analyze_moe_model import analyze_moe_model
179	
180	    moe_result = None  # Store for later use when registering quantized model
181	    try:
182	        moe_result = analyze_moe_model(str(input_path), use_cache=True)
183	        if not moe_result or not moe_result.get("is_moe"):
184	            print_error("Only MoE models can be quantized to AMX format")
185	            console.print()
186	            console.print(f"  The model at {input_path} is not a MoE model.")
187	            console.print("  AMX quantization is designed for MoE models (e.g., DeepSeek-V3).")
188	            raise typer.Exit(1)
189	    except Exception as e:
190	        print_warning(f"Could not detect MoE information: {e}")
191	        console.print()
192	        if not yes:
193	            if not confirm("Continue quantization anyway?", default=False):
194	                raise typer.Exit(1)
195	
196	    # Detect CPU configuration and resolve output path (only needed in non-interactive mode)
197	    if not is_interactive:
198	        print_info(t("quant_input_path", path=str(input_path)))
199	
200	        # Detect CPU configuration (needed for output path)
201	        cpu = detect_cpu_info()
202	        final_cpu_threads = cpu_threads or cpu.cores
203	        final_numa_nodes = numa_nodes or cpu.numa_nodes
204	
205	        # Resolve output path
206	        if output is None:
207	            # Priority: paths.weights > paths.models[0] > model's parent directory
208	            weights_dir = settings.weights_dir
209	
210	            if weights_dir and weights_dir.exists():
211	                # Use configured weights directory (highest priority)
212	                output = weights_dir / f"{input_path.name}-AMX{method.value.upper()}-NUMA{final_numa_nodes}"
213	            else:
214	                # Use first model storage path
215	                model_paths = settings.get_model_paths()
216	                if model_paths and model_paths[0].exists():
217	                    output = model_paths[0] / f"{input_path.name}-AMX{method.value.upper()}-NUMA{final_numa_nodes}"
218	                else:
219	                    # Fallback to model's parent directory
220	                    output = input_path.parent / f"{input_path.name}-AMX{method.value.upper()}-NUMA{final_numa_nodes}"
221	
222	        print_info(t("quant_output_path", path=str(output)))
223	        print_info(t("quant_method", method=method.value.upper()))
224	        print_info(t("quant_cpu_threads", threads=final_cpu_threads))
225	        print_info(t("quant_numa_nodes", nodes=final_numa_nodes))
226	
227	        # Calculate space requirements
228	        console.print()
229	        console.print(f"[bold cyan]{t('quant_disk_analysis')}[/bold cyan]")
230	        console.print()
231	
232	        # Calculate source model size
233	        try:
234	            total_bytes = sum(f.stat().st_size for f in input_path.glob("*.safetensors") if f.is_file())
235	            source_size_gb = total_bytes / (1024**3)
236	        except Exception:
237	            source_size_gb = 0.0
238	
239	        # Estimate quantized size
240	        input_bits = {"fp8": 8, "fp16": 16, "bf16": 16}
241	        quant_bits = {"int4": 4, "int8": 8}
242	        input_bit = input_bits.get(input_type, 16)
243	        quant_bit = quant_bits.get(method.value, 4)
244	        ratio = quant_bit / input_bit
245	        estimated_size_gb = source_size_gb * ratio
246	
247	        # Check available space
248	        import shutil
249	
250	        try:
251	            check_path = output.parent if not output.exists() else output
252	            while not check_path.exists() and check_path != check_path.parent:
253	                check_path = check_path.parent
254	            stat = shutil.disk_usage(check_path)
255	            available_gb = stat.free / (1024**3)
256	        except Exception:
257	            available_gb = 0.0
258	
259	        is_sufficient = available_gb >= (estimated_size_gb * 1.2)
260	
261	        console.print(f"  {t('quant_source_size'):<26} {source_size_gb:.2f} GB")
262	        console.print(f"  {t('quant_estimated_size'):<26} {estimated_size_gb:.2f} GB")
263	        console.print(f"  {t('quant_available_space'):<26} {available_gb:.2f} GB")
264	        console.print()
265	
266	        if not is_sufficient:
267	            required_with_buffer = estimated_size_gb * 1.2
268	            print_warning(t("quant_insufficient_space"))
269	            console.print()
270	            console.print(f"  {t('quant_required_space'):<26} {required_with_buffer:.2f} GB")
271	            console.print(f"  {t('quant_available_space'):<26} {available_gb:.2f} GB")
272	            console.print(f"  {t('quant_shortage'):<26} {required_with_buffer - available_gb:.2f} GB")
273	            console.print()
274	            console.print(f"  {t('quant_may_fail')}")
275	            console.print()
276	
277	            if not yes:
278	                if not confirm(t("quant_continue_anyway"), default=False):
279	                    raise typer.Abort()
280	            console.print()
281	
282	        # Check if output exists and generate unique name
283	        if output.exists():
284	            print_warning(t("quant_output_exists", path=str(output)))
285	            console.print()
286	
287	            # Generate unique name by adding suffix
288	            original_name = output.name
289	            parent_dir = output.parent
290	            counter = 2
291	
292	            while output.exists():
293	                new_name = f"{original_name}-{counter}"
294	                output = parent_dir / new_name
295	                counter += 1
296	
297	            print_success(t("quant_using_unique", path=str(output)))
298	            console.print()
299	
300	        # Confirm (only show if not using --yes flag)
301	        if not yes:
302	            console.print()
303	            print_warning(t("quant_time_warning"))
304	            console.print()
305	
306	            if not confirm(t("prompt_continue")):
307	                raise typer.Abort()
308	    else:
309	        # Interactive mode: cpu_threads and numa_nodes already set
310	        final_cpu_threads = cpu_threads
311	        final_numa_nodes = numa_nodes
312	
313	    # Find conversion script
314	    kt_kernel_path = _find_kt_kernel_path()
315	    if kt_kernel_path is None:
316	        print_error("kt-kernel not found. Install with: kt install inference")
317	        raise typer.Exit(1)
318	
319	    script_path = kt_kernel_path / "scripts" / "convert_cpu_weights.py"
320	    if not script_path.exists():
321	        print_error(f"Conversion script not found: {script_path}")
322	        raise typer.Exit(1)
323	
324	    # Build command
325	    cmd = [
326	        sys.executable,
327	        str(script_path),
328	        "--input-path",
329	        str(input_path),
330	        "--input-type",
331	        input_type,
332	        "--output",
333	        str(output),
334	        "--quant-method",
335	        method.value,
336	        "--cpuinfer-threads",
337	        str(final_cpu_threads),
338	        "--threadpool-count",
339	        str(final_numa_nodes),
340	    ]
341	
342	    if no_merge:
343	        cmd.append("--no-merge-safetensor")
344	
345	    if gpu:
346	        cmd.append("--gpu")
347	
348	    # Run quantization
349	    console.print()
350	    print_step(t("quant_starting"))
351	    console.print()
352	    console.print(f"[dim]$ {' '.join(cmd)}[/dim]")
353	    console.print()
354	    console.print("[dim]" + "=" * 80 + "[/dim]")
355	    console.print()
356	
357	    try:
358	        # Run with real-time stdout/stderr output
359	        import os
360	        import time
361	
362	        env = os.environ.copy()
363	        env["PYTHONUNBUFFERED"] = "1"  # Disable Python output buffering
364	
365	        # Record start time
366	        start_time = time.time()
367	
368	        process = subprocess.run(
369	            cmd,
370	            stdout=None,  # Inherit parent's stdout (real-time output)
371	            stderr=None,  # Inherit parent's stderr (real-time output)
372	            env=env,
373	        )
374	
375	        # Calculate elapsed time
376	        elapsed_time = time.time() - start_time
377	        hours = int(elapsed_time // 3600)
378	        minutes = int((elapsed_time % 3600) // 60)
379	        seconds = int(elapsed_time % 60)
380	
381	        console.print()
382	        console.print("[dim]" + "=" * 80 + "[/dim]")
383	        console.print()
384	
385	        if process.returncode == 0:
386	            print_success(t("quant_complete"))
387	            console.print()
388	
389	            # Display elapsed time
390	            if hours > 0:
391	                time_str = f"{hours}h {minutes}m {seconds}s"
392	            elif minutes > 0:
393	                time_str = f"{minutes}m {seconds}s"
394	            else:
395	                time_str = f"{seconds}s"
396	            console.print(f"  [cyan]{t('quant_time_elapsed')} {time_str}[/cyan]")
397	            console.print()
398	            console.print(f"  Quantized weights saved to: {output}")
399	            console.print()
400	
401	            # Auto-register the quantized model
402	            try:
403	                from kt_kernel.cli.utils.user_model_registry import UserModel
404	
405	                # Generate model name from output path
406	                base_name = output.name
407	                suggested_name = user_registry.suggest_name(base_name)
408	
409	                # Determine MoE information and source model name
410	                if user_model_obj:
411	                    is_moe_val = user_model_obj.is_moe
412	                    num_experts = user_model_obj.moe_num_experts
413	                    num_active = user_model_obj.moe_num_experts_per_tok
414	                    repo_type_val = user_model_obj.repo_type
415	                    repo_id_val = user_model_obj.repo_id
416	                    source_model_name = user_model_obj.name  # Store source model name
417	                elif moe_result:
418	                    is_moe_val = moe_result.get("is_moe", True)
419	                    num_experts = moe_result.get("num_experts")
420	                    num_active = moe_result.get("num_experts_per_tok")
421	                    repo_type_val = None
422	                    repo_id_val = None
423	                    source_model_name = input_path.name  # Use folder name as fallback
424	                else:
425	                    is_moe_val = None
426	                    num_experts = None
427	                    num_active = None
428	                    repo_type_val = None
429	                    repo_id_val = None
430	                    source_model_name = input_path.name  # Use folder name as fallback
431	
432	                # Create new model entry (AMX format uses "safetensors" format, detected by is_amx_weights())
433	                new_model = UserModel(
434	                    name=suggested_name,
435	                    path=str(output),
436	                    format="safetensors",  # AMX files are safetensors format
437	                    repo_type=repo_type_val,
438	                    repo_id=repo_id_val,
439	                    sha256_status="not_checked",  # AMX weights don't need verification
440	                    # Inherit MoE information from source model
441	                    is_moe=is_moe_val,
442	                    moe_num_experts=num_experts,
443	                    moe_num_experts_per_tok=num_active,
444	                    # AMX quantization metadata
445	                    amx_source_model=source_model_name,
446	                    amx_quant_method=method.value,  # "int4" or "int8"
447	                    amx_numa_nodes=final_numa_nodes,
448	                )
449	
450	                user_registry.add_model(new_model)
451	                console.print()
452	                print_success(t("quant_registered", name=suggested_name))
453	                console.print()
454	                console.print(f"  {t('quant_view_with')} [cyan]kt model list[/cyan]")
455	                console.print(f"  {t('quant_use_with')}  [cyan]kt run {suggested_name}[/cyan]")
456	                console.print()
457	            except Exception as e:
458	                # Non-fatal error - quantization succeeded but registration failed
459	                console.print()
460	                print_warning(t("quant_register_failed", error=str(e)))
461	                console.print()
462	                console.print(f"  {t('quant_use_with')}")
463	                console.print(f"    kt run {model} --weights-path {output}")
464	                console.print()
465	        else:
466	            print_error(f"Quantization failed with exit code {process.returncode}")
467	            raise typer.Exit(process.returncode)
468	
469	    except FileNotFoundError as e:
470	        print_error(f"Failed to run quantization: {e}")
471	        raise typer.Exit(1)
472	    except KeyboardInterrupt:
473	        console.print()
474	        print_warning("Quantization interrupted.")
475	        raise typer.Exit(130)
476	
477	
478	def _resolve_input_path(model: str, settings) -> Optional[Path]:
479	    """Resolve the input model path."""
480	    # Check if it's already a path
481	    path = Path(model)
482	    if path.exists() and (path / "config.json").exists():
483	        return path
484	
485	    # Search in models directory
486	    from kt_kernel.cli.utils.model_registry import get_registry
487	
488	    registry = get_registry()
489	    matches = registry.search(model)
490	
491	    if matches:
492	        model_info = matches[0]
493	        # Try to find in all configured model directories
494	        model_paths = settings.get_model_paths()
495	
496	        for models_dir in model_paths:
497	            possible_paths = [
498	                models_dir / model_info.name,
499	                models_dir / model_info.name.lower(),
500	                models_dir / model_info.hf_repo.split("/")[-1],
501	            ]
502	
503	            for p in possible_paths:
504	                if p.exists() and (p / "config.json").exists():
505	                    return p
506	
507	    return None
508	
509	
510	def _find_kt_kernel_path() -> Optional[Path]:
511	    """Find the kt-kernel installation path."""
512	    try:
513	        import kt_kernel
514	
515	        return Path(kt_kernel.__file__).parent.parent
516	    except ImportError:
517	        pass
518	
519	    # Check common locations
520	    possible_paths = [
521	        Path.home() / "Projects" / "ktransformers" / "kt-kernel",
522	        Path.cwd().parent / "kt-kernel",
523	        Path.cwd() / "kt-kernel",
524	    ]
525	
526	    for path in possible_paths:
527	        if path.exists() and (path / "scripts").exists():
528	            return path
529	
530	    return None

1	"""
2	Bench commands for kt-cli.
3	
4	Runs benchmarks for performance testing.
5	"""
6	
7	import subprocess
8	import sys
9	from enum import Enum
10	from pathlib import Path
11	from typing import Optional
12	
13	import typer
14	
15	from kt_kernel.cli.i18n import t
16	from kt_kernel.cli.utils.console import (
17	    console,
18	    print_error,
19	    print_info,
20	    print_step,
21	    print_success,
22	)
23	
24	
25	class BenchType(str, Enum):
26	    """Benchmark type."""
27	
28	    INFERENCE = "inference"
29	    MLA = "mla"
30	    MOE = "moe"
31	    LINEAR = "linear"
32	    ATTENTION = "attention"
33	    ALL = "all"
34	
35	
36	def bench(
37	    type: BenchType = typer.Option(
38	        BenchType.ALL,
39	        "--type",
40	        "-t",
41	        help="Benchmark type",
42	    ),
43	    model: Optional[str] = typer.Option(
44	        None,
45	        "--model",
46	        "-m",
47	        help="Model to benchmark",
48	    ),
49	    output: Optional[Path] = typer.Option(
50	        None,
51	        "--output",
52	        "-o",
53	        help="Output file for results (JSON)",
54	    ),
55	    iterations: int = typer.Option(
56	        10,
57	        "--iterations",
58	        "-n",
59	        help="Number of iterations",
60	    ),
61	) -> None:
62	    """Run full benchmark suite."""
63	    console.print()
64	    print_step(t("bench_starting"))
65	    print_info(t("bench_type", type=type.value))
66	    console.print()
67	
68	    if type == BenchType.ALL:
69	        _run_all_benchmarks(model, output, iterations)
70	    elif type == BenchType.INFERENCE:
71	        _run_inference_benchmark(model, output, iterations)
72	    elif type == BenchType.MLA:
73	        _run_component_benchmark("mla", output, iterations)
74	    elif type == BenchType.MOE:
75	        _run_component_benchmark("moe", output, iterations)
76	    elif type == BenchType.LINEAR:
77	        _run_component_benchmark("linear", output, iterations)
78	    elif type == BenchType.ATTENTION:
79	        _run_component_benchmark("attention", output, iterations)
80	
81	    console.print()
82	    print_success(t("bench_complete"))
83	    if output:
84	        console.print(f"  Results saved to: {output}")
85	    console.print()
86	
87	
88	def microbench(
89	    component: str = typer.Argument(
90	        "moe",
91	        help="Component to benchmark (moe, mla, linear, attention)",
92	    ),
93	    batch_size: int = typer.Option(
94	        1,
95	        "--batch-size",
96	        "-b",
97	        help="Batch size",
98	    ),
99	    seq_len: int = typer.Option(
100	        1,
101	        "--seq-len",
102	        "-s",
103	        help="Sequence length",
104	    ),
105	    iterations: int = typer.Option(
106	        100,
107	        "--iterations",
108	        "-n",
109	        help="Number of iterations",
110	    ),
111	    warmup: int = typer.Option(
112	        10,
113	        "--warmup",
114	        "-w",
115	        help="Warmup iterations",
116	    ),
117	    output: Optional[Path] = typer.Option(
118	        None,
119	        "--output",
120	        "-o",
121	        help="Output file for results (JSON)",
122	    ),
123	) -> None:
124	    """Run micro-benchmark for specific components."""
125	    console.print()
126	    console.print(f"[yellow]{t('feature_coming_soon')}[/yellow]")
127	    console.print()
128	    raise typer.Exit(0)
129	
130	    # Try to find the benchmark script
131	    kt_kernel_path = _find_kt_kernel_path()
132	
133	    if kt_kernel_path is None:
134	        print_error("kt-kernel not found. Install with: kt install inference")
135	        raise typer.Exit(1)
136	
137	    bench_dir = kt_kernel_path / "bench"
138	
139	    # Map component to script
140	    component_scripts = {
141	        "moe": "bench_moe.py",
142	        "mla": "bench_mla.py",
143	        "linear": "bench_linear.py",
144	        "attention": "bench_attention.py",
145	        "mlp": "bench_mlp.py",
146	    }
147	
148	    script_name = component_scripts.get(component.lower())
149	    if script_name is None:
150	        print_error(f"Unknown component: {component}")
151	        console.print(f"Available: {', '.join(component_scripts.keys())}")
152	        raise typer.Exit(1)
153	
154	    script_path = bench_dir / script_name
155	    if not script_path.exists():
156	        print_error(f"Benchmark script not found: {script_path}")
157	        raise typer.Exit(1)
158	
159	    # Run benchmark
160	    cmd = [
161	        sys.executable,
162	        str(script_path),
163	        "--batch-size",
164	        str(batch_size),
165	        "--seq-len",
166	        str(seq_len),
167	        "--iterations",
168	        str(iterations),
169	        "--warmup",
170	        str(warmup),
171	    ]
172	
173	    if output:
174	        cmd.extend(["--output", str(output)])
175	
176	    console.print(f"[dim]$ {' '.join(cmd)}[/dim]")
177	    console.print()
178	
179	    try:
180	        process = subprocess.run(cmd)
181	
182	        if process.returncode == 0:
183	            console.print()
184	            print_success(t("bench_complete"))
185	            if output:
186	                console.print(f"  Results saved to: {output}")
187	        else:
188	            print_error(f"Benchmark failed with exit code {process.returncode}")
189	            raise typer.Exit(process.returncode)
190	
191	    except FileNotFoundError as e:
192	        print_error(f"Failed to run benchmark: {e}")
193	        raise typer.Exit(1)
194	
195	
196	def _find_kt_kernel_path() -> Optional[Path]:
197	    """Find the kt-kernel installation path."""
198	    try:
199	        import kt_kernel
200	
201	        return Path(kt_kernel.__file__).parent.parent
202	    except ImportError:
203	        pass
204	
205	    # Check common locations
206	    possible_paths = [
207	        Path.home() / "Projects" / "ktransformers" / "kt-kernel",
208	        Path("/opt/ktransformers/kt-kernel"),
209	        Path.cwd() / "kt-kernel",
210	    ]
211	
212	    for path in possible_paths:
213	        if path.exists() and (path / "bench").exists():
214	            return path
215	
216	    return None
217	
218	
219	def _run_all_benchmarks(model: Optional[str], output: Optional[Path], iterations: int) -> None:
220	    """Run all benchmarks."""
221	    components = ["moe", "mla", "linear", "attention"]
222	
223	    for component in components:
224	        console.print(f"\n[bold]Running {component} benchmark...[/bold]")
225	        _run_component_benchmark(component, None, iterations)
226	
227	
228	def _run_inference_benchmark(model: Optional[str], output: Optional[Path], iterations: int) -> None:
229	    """Run inference benchmark."""
230	    if model is None:
231	        print_error("Model required for inference benchmark. Use --model flag.")
232	        raise typer.Exit(1)
233	
234	    print_info(f"Running inference benchmark on {model}...")
235	    console.print()
236	    console.print("[dim]This will start the server and run test requests.[/dim]")
237	    console.print()
238	
239	    # TODO: Implement actual inference benchmarking
240	    print_error("Inference benchmarking not yet implemented.")
241	
242	
243	def _run_component_benchmark(component: str, output: Optional[Path], iterations: int) -> None:
244	    """Run a component benchmark."""
245	    kt_kernel_path = _find_kt_kernel_path()
246	
247	    if kt_kernel_path is None:
248	        print_error("kt-kernel not found.")
249	        return
250	
251	    bench_dir = kt_kernel_path / "bench"
252	    script_map = {
253	        "moe": "bench_moe.py",
254	        "mla": "bench_mla.py",
255	        "linear": "bench_linear.py",
256	        "attention": "bench_attention.py",
257	    }
258	
259	    script_name = script_map.get(component)
260	    if script_name is None:
261	        print_error(f"Unknown component: {component}")
262	        return
263	
264	    script_path = bench_dir / script_name
265	    if not script_path.exists():
266	        print_error(f"Script not found: {script_path}")
267	        return
268	
269	    cmd = [sys.executable, str(script_path), "--iterations", str(iterations)]
270	
271	    try:
272	        subprocess.run(cmd)
273	    except Exception as e:
274	        print_error(f"Benchmark failed: {e}")

1	"""
2	Version command for kt-cli.
3	
4	Displays version information for kt-cli and related packages.
5	"""
6	
7	import platform
8	from typing import Optional
9	
10	import typer
11	
12	from kt_kernel.cli import __version__
13	from kt_kernel.cli.i18n import t
14	from kt_kernel.cli.utils.console import console, print_version_table
15	from kt_kernel.cli.utils.environment import detect_cuda_version, get_installed_package_version
16	
17	
18	def _get_sglang_info() -> str:
19	    """Get sglang-kt version and installation source information."""
20	    from kt_kernel.cli.utils.sglang_checker import check_sglang_installation
21	
22	    info = check_sglang_installation()
23	
24	    if not info["installed"]:
25	        return t("version_not_installed")
26	
27	    # Get version from package metadata (prefer sglang-kt)
28	    version = get_installed_package_version("sglang-kt")
29	    if not version:
30	        version = get_installed_package_version("sglang")
31	    if not version:
32	        version = info.get("version") or "unknown"
33	
34	    # Determine source label
35	    if info.get("is_kvcache_fork"):
36	        if info["from_source"] and info.get("git_info"):
37	            git_remote = info["git_info"].get("remote", "")
38	            return f"{version} [dim](Source: {git_remote})[/dim]"
39	        elif info["editable"]:
40	            return f"{version} [dim](editable)[/dim]"
41	        else:
42	            return f"{version} [dim](sglang-kt)[/dim]"
43	    elif info["from_source"]:
44	        if info.get("git_info"):
45	            git_remote = info["git_info"].get("remote", "")
46	            return f"{version} [dim](Source: {git_remote})[/dim]"
47	        return f"{version} [dim](source)[/dim]"
48	    else:
49	        return f"{version} [dim](PyPI)[/dim]"
50	
51	
52	def version(
53	    verbose: bool = typer.Option(False, "--verbose", "-v", help="Show detailed version info"),
54	) -> None:
55	    """Show version information."""
56	    console.print(f"\n[bold]{t('version_info')}[/bold] v{__version__}\n")
57	
58	    # Basic info
59	    versions = {
60	        t("version_python"): platform.python_version(),
61	        t("version_platform"): f"{platform.system()} {platform.release()}",
62	    }
63	
64	    # CUDA version
65	    cuda_version = detect_cuda_version()
66	    versions[t("version_cuda")] = cuda_version or t("version_cuda_not_found")
67	
68	    print_version_table(versions)
69	
70	    # Always show key packages with installation source
71	    console.print("\n[bold]Packages:[/bold]\n")
72	
73	    sglang_info = _get_sglang_info()
74	    key_packages = {
75	        t("version_kt_kernel"): get_installed_package_version("kt-kernel") or t("version_not_installed"),
76	        t("version_sglang"): sglang_info,
77	    }
78	
79	    print_version_table(key_packages)
80	
81	    # Show SGLang installation hint if not installed
82	    if sglang_info == t("version_not_installed"):
83	        from kt_kernel.cli.utils.sglang_checker import print_sglang_install_instructions
84	
85	        console.print()
86	        print_sglang_install_instructions()
87	
88	    if verbose:
89	        console.print("\n[bold]Additional Packages:[/bold]\n")
90	
91	        package_versions = {
92	            t("version_ktransformers"): get_installed_package_version("ktransformers") or t("version_not_installed"),
93	            t("version_llamafactory"): get_installed_package_version("llamafactory") or t("version_not_installed"),
94	            "typer": get_installed_package_version("typer") or t("version_not_installed"),
95	            "rich": get_installed_package_version("rich") or t("version_not_installed"),
96	            "torch": get_installed_package_version("torch") or t("version_not_installed"),
97	            "transformers": get_installed_package_version("transformers") or t("version_not_installed"),
98	        }
99	
100	        print_version_table(package_versions)
101	
102	    console.print()
103

1	"""
2	Configuration management for kt-cli.
3	
4	Handles reading and writing configuration from ~/.ktransformers/config.yaml
5	"""
6	
7	import os
8	from pathlib import Path
9	from typing import Any, Optional
10	
11	import yaml
12	
13	# Default configuration directory
14	DEFAULT_CONFIG_DIR = Path.home() / ".ktransformers"
15	DEFAULT_CONFIG_FILE = DEFAULT_CONFIG_DIR / "config.yaml"
16	DEFAULT_MODELS_DIR = DEFAULT_CONFIG_DIR / "models"
17	DEFAULT_CACHE_DIR = DEFAULT_CONFIG_DIR / "cache"
18	
19	# Default configuration values
20	DEFAULT_CONFIG = {
21	    "general": {
22	        "language": "auto",  # auto, en, zh
23	        "color": True,
24	        "verbose": False,
25	    },
26	    "paths": {
27	        "models": str(DEFAULT_MODELS_DIR),
28	        "cache": str(DEFAULT_CACHE_DIR),
29	        "weights": "",  # Custom quantized weights path
30	    },
31	    "server": {
32	        "host": "0.0.0.0",
33	        "port": 30000,
34	    },
35	    "inference": {
36	        # Inference parameters are model-specific and should not have defaults
37	        # They will be auto-detected or use model-specific optimizations
38	        # Environment variables (general optimizations)
39	        "env": {
40	            "PYTORCH_ALLOC_CONF": "expandable_segments:True",
41	            "SGLANG_ENABLE_JIT_DEEPGEMM": "0",
42	        },
43	    },
44	    "download": {
45	        "mirror": "",  # HuggingFace mirror URL
46	        "resume": True,
47	        "verify": True,
48	    },
49	    "advanced": {
50	        # Environment variables to set when running
51	        "env": {},
52	        # Extra arguments to pass to sglang
53	        "sglang_args": [],
54	        # Extra arguments to pass to llamafactory
55	        "llamafactory_args": [],
56	    },
57	    "dependencies": {
58	        # SGLang installation source configuration
59	        "sglang": {
60	            "source": "github",  # "pypi" or "github"
61	            "repo": "https://github.com/kvcache-ai/sglang",
62	            "branch": "main",
63	        },
64	    },
65	}
66	
67	
68	class Settings:
69	    """Configuration manager for kt-cli."""
70	
71	    def __init__(self, config_path: Optional[Path] = None):
72	        """Initialize settings manager.
73	
74	        Args:
75	            config_path: Path to config file. Defaults to ~/.ktransformers/config.yaml
76	        """
77	        self.config_path = config_path or DEFAULT_CONFIG_FILE
78	        self.config_dir = self.config_path.parent
79	        self._config: dict[str, Any] = {}
80	        self._load()
81	
82	    def _ensure_dirs(self) -> None:
83	        """Ensure configuration directories exist."""
84	        self.config_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
85	
86	        # Ensure all model paths exist
87	        model_paths = self.get_model_paths()
88	        for path in model_paths:
89	            path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
90	
91	        Path(self.get("paths.cache", DEFAULT_CACHE_DIR)).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
92	
93	    def _load(self) -> None:
94	        """Load configuration from file."""
95	        self._config = self._deep_copy(DEFAULT_CONFIG)
96	
97	        if self.config_path.exists():
98	            try:
99	                with open(self.config_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
100	                    user_config = yaml.safe_load(f) or {}
101	                self._deep_merge(self._config, user_config)
102	            except (yaml.YAMLError, OSError) as e:
103	                # Log warning but continue with defaults
104	                print(f"Warning: Failed to load config: {e}")
105	
106	        self._ensure_dirs()
107	
108	    def _save(self) -> None:
109	        """Save configuration to file."""
110	        self._ensure_dirs()
111	        try:
112	            with open(self.config_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
113	                yaml.dump(self._config, f, default_flow_style=False, allow_unicode=True)
114	        except OSError as e:
115	            raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to save config: {e}")
116	
117	    def _deep_copy(self, obj: Any) -> Any:
118	        """Create a deep copy of a nested dict."""
119	        if isinstance(obj, dict):
120	            return {k: self._deep_copy(v) for k, v in obj.items()}
121	        if isinstance(obj, list):
122	            return [self._deep_copy(item) for item in obj]
123	        return obj
124	
125	    def _deep_merge(self, base: dict, override: dict) -> None:
126	        """Deep merge override into base."""
127	        for key, value in override.items():
128	            if key in base and isinstance(base[key], dict) and isinstance(value, dict):
129	                self._deep_merge(base[key], value)
130	            else:
131	                base[key] = value
132	
133	    def get(self, key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
134	        """Get a configuration value by dot-separated key.
135	
136	        Args:
137	            key: Dot-separated key path (e.g., "server.port")
138	            default: Default value if key not found
139	
140	        Returns:
141	            Configuration value or default
142	        """
143	        parts = key.split(".")
144	        value = self._config
145	
146	        for part in parts:
147	            if isinstance(value, dict) and part in value:
148	                value = value[part]
149	            else:
150	                return default
151	
152	        return value
153	
154	    def set(self, key: str, value: Any) -> None:
155	        """Set a configuration value by dot-separated key.
156	
157	        Args:
158	            key: Dot-separated key path (e.g., "server.port")
159	            value: Value to set
160	        """
161	        parts = key.split(".")
162	        config = self._config
163	
164	        # Navigate to parent
165	        for part in parts[:-1]:
166	            if part not in config:
167	                config[part] = {}
168	            config = config[part]
169	
170	        # Set value
171	        config[parts[-1]] = value
172	        self._save()
173	
174	    def delete(self, key: str) -> bool:
175	        """Delete a configuration value.
176	
177	        Args:
178	            key: Dot-separated key path
179	
180	        Returns:
181	            True if key was deleted, False if not found
182	        """
183	        parts = key.split(".")
184	        config = self._config
185	
186	        # Navigate to parent
187	        for part in parts[:-1]:
188	            if part not in config:
189	                return False
190	            config = config[part]
191	
192	        # Delete key
193	        if parts[-1] in config:
194	            del config[parts[-1]]
195	            self._save()
196	            return True
197	        return False
198	
199	    def reset(self) -> None:
200	        """Reset configuration to defaults."""
201	        self._config = self._deep_copy(DEFAULT_CONFIG)
202	        self._save()
203	
204	    def get_all(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
205	        """Get all configuration values."""
206	        return self._deep_copy(self._config)
207	
208	    def get_env_vars(self) -> dict[str, str]:
209	        """Get environment variables to set."""
210	        env_vars = {}
211	
212	        # Get from advanced.env
213	        advanced_env = self.get("advanced.env", {})
214	        if isinstance(advanced_env, dict):
215	            env_vars.update({k: str(v) for k, v in advanced_env.items()})
216	
217	        return env_vars
218	
219	    @property
220	    def models_dir(self) -> Path:
221	        """Get the primary models directory path (for backward compatibility)."""
222	        paths = self.get_model_paths()
223	        return paths[0] if paths else Path(DEFAULT_MODELS_DIR)
224	
225	    def get_model_paths(self) -> list[Path]:
226	        """Get all model directory paths.
227	
228	        Returns a list of Path objects. Supports both:
229	        - Single path: paths.models = "/path/to/models"
230	        - Multiple paths: paths.models = ["/path/1", "/path/2"]
231	        """
232	        models_config = self.get("paths.models", DEFAULT_MODELS_DIR)
233	
234	        # Handle both string and list
235	        if isinstance(models_config, str):
236	            return [Path(models_config)]
237	        elif isinstance(models_config, list):
238	            return [Path(p) for p in models_config]
239	        else:
240	            return [Path(DEFAULT_MODELS_DIR)]
241	
242	    def add_model_path(self, path: str) -> None:
243	        """Add a new model path to the configuration."""
244	        models_config = self.get("paths.models", DEFAULT_MODELS_DIR)
245	
246	        # Convert to list if it's a string
247	        if isinstance(models_config, str):
248	            paths = [models_config]
249	        elif isinstance(models_config, list):
250	            paths = list(models_config)
251	        else:
252	            paths = []
253	
254	        # Add new path if not already present
255	        if path not in paths:
256	            paths.append(path)
257	            self.set("paths.models", paths)
258	
259	    def remove_model_path(self, path: str) -> bool:
260	        """Remove a model path from the configuration.
261	
262	        Returns True if path was removed, False if not found.
263	        """
264	        models_config = self.get("paths.models", DEFAULT_MODELS_DIR)
265	
266	        if isinstance(models_config, str):
267	            # Can't remove if it's a single string
268	            if models_config == path:
269	                # Don't remove the last path
270	                return False
271	            return False
272	        elif isinstance(models_config, list):
273	            if path in models_config:
274	                paths = list(models_config)
275	                paths.remove(path)
276	                # Don't allow removing all paths
277	                if not paths:
278	                    return False
279	                self.set("paths.models", paths if len(paths) > 1 else paths[0])
280	                return True
281	
282	        return False
283	
284	    @property
285	    def cache_dir(self) -> Path:
286	        """Get the cache directory path."""
287	        return Path(self.get("paths.cache", DEFAULT_CACHE_DIR))
288	
289	    @property
290	    def weights_dir(self) -> Optional[Path]:
291	        """Get the custom weights directory path."""
292	        weights = self.get("paths.weights", "")
293	        return Path(weights) if weights else None
294	
295	
296	# Global settings instance
297	_settings: Optional[Settings] = None
298	
299	
300	def get_settings() -> Settings:
301	    """Get the global settings instance."""
302	    global _settings
303	    if _settings is None:
304	        _settings = Settings()
305	    return _settings
306	
307	
308	def reset_settings() -> None:
309	    """Reset the global settings instance."""
310	    global _settings
311	    _settings = None
312

1	"""
2	User Model Registry
3	
4	Manages user-registered models in ~/.ktransformers/user_models.yaml
5	"""
6	
7	from dataclasses import dataclass, asdict, field
8	from datetime import datetime
9	from pathlib import Path
10	from typing import Optional, List, Dict, Any
11	import yaml
12	
13	
14	# Constants
15	USER_MODELS_FILE = Path.home() / ".ktransformers" / "user_models.yaml"
16	REGISTRY_VERSION = "1.0"
17	
18	
19	@dataclass
20	class UserModel:
21	    """Represents a user-registered model"""
22	
23	    name: str  # User-editable name (default: folder name)
24	    path: str  # Absolute path to model directory
25	    format: str  # "safetensors" | "gguf"
26	    id: Optional[str] = None  # Unique UUID for this model (auto-generated if None)
27	    repo_type: Optional[str] = None  # "huggingface" | "modelscope" | None
28	    repo_id: Optional[str] = None  # e.g., "deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3"
29	    sha256_status: str = "not_checked"  # "not_checked" | "checking" | "passed" | "failed" | "no_repo"
30	    gpu_model_ids: Optional[List[str]] = None  # For llamafile/AMX: list of GPU model UUIDs to run with
31	    created_at: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().isoformat())
32	    last_verified: Optional[str] = None  # ISO format datetime
33	    # MoE information (cached from analyze_moe_model)
34	    is_moe: Optional[bool] = None  # True if MoE model, False if non-MoE, None if not analyzed
35	    moe_num_experts: Optional[int] = None  # Total number of experts (for MoE models)
36	    moe_num_experts_per_tok: Optional[int] = None  # Number of active experts per token (for MoE models)
37	    # AMX quantization metadata (for format == "amx")
38	    amx_source_model: Optional[str] = None  # Name of the source MoE model that was quantized
39	    amx_quant_method: Optional[str] = None  # "int4" | "int8"
40	    amx_numa_nodes: Optional[int] = None  # Number of NUMA nodes used for quantization
41	
42	    def __post_init__(self):
43	        """Ensure ID is set after initialization"""
44	        if self.id is None:
45	            import uuid
46	
47	            self.id = str(uuid.uuid4())
48	
49	    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
50	        """Convert to dictionary for YAML serialization"""
51	        return asdict(self)
52	
53	    @classmethod
54	    def from_dict(cls, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> "UserModel":
55	        """Create from dictionary loaded from YAML"""
56	        return cls(**data)
57	
58	    def path_exists(self) -> bool:
59	        """Check if model path still exists"""
60	        return Path(self.path).exists()
61	
62	
63	class UserModelRegistry:
64	    """Manages the user model registry"""
65	
66	    def __init__(self, registry_file: Optional[Path] = None):
67	        """
68	        Initialize the registry
69	
70	        Args:
71	            registry_file: Path to the registry YAML file (default: USER_MODELS_FILE)
72	        """
73	        self.registry_file = registry_file or USER_MODELS_FILE
74	        self.models: List[UserModel] = []
75	        self.version = REGISTRY_VERSION
76	
77	        # Ensure directory exists
78	        self.registry_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
79	
80	        # Load existing registry
81	        self.load()
82	
83	    def load(self) -> None:
84	        """Load models from YAML file"""
85	        if not self.registry_file.exists():
86	            # Initialize empty registry
87	            self.models = []
88	            self.save()  # Create the file
89	            return
90	
91	        try:
92	            with open(self.registry_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
93	                data = yaml.safe_load(f)
94	
95	            if not data:
96	                self.models = []
97	                return
98	
99	            # Load version
100	            self.version = data.get("version", REGISTRY_VERSION)
101	
102	            # Load models
103	            models_data = data.get("models", [])
104	            self.models = [UserModel.from_dict(m) for m in models_data]
105	
106	            # Migrate: ensure all models have UUIDs (for backward compatibility)
107	            needs_save = False
108	            for model in self.models:
109	                if model.id is None:
110	                    import uuid
111	
112	                    model.id = str(uuid.uuid4())
113	                    needs_save = True
114	
115	            if needs_save:
116	                self.save()
117	
118	        except Exception as e:
119	            raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to load user model registry: {e}")
120	
121	    def save(self) -> None:
122	        """Save models to YAML file"""
123	        data = {"version": self.version, "models": [m.to_dict() for m in self.models]}
124	
125	        try:
126	            with open(self.registry_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
127	                yaml.safe_dump(data, f, default_flow_style=False, allow_unicode=True, sort_keys=False)
128	        except Exception as e:
129	            raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to save user model registry: {e}")
130	
131	    def add_model(self, model: UserModel) -> None:
132	        """
133	        Add a model to the registry
134	
135	        Args:
136	            model: UserModel instance to add
137	
138	        Raises:
139	            ValueError: If a model with the same name already exists
140	        """
141	        if self.check_name_conflict(model.name):
142	            raise ValueError(f"Model with name '{model.name}' already exists")
143	
144	        self.models.append(model)
145	        self.save()
146	
147	    def remove_model(self, name: str) -> bool:
148	        """
149	        Remove a model from the registry
150	
151	        Args:
152	            name: Name of the model to remove
153	
154	        Returns:
155	            True if model was removed, False if not found
156	        """
157	        original_count = len(self.models)
158	        self.models = [m for m in self.models if m.name != name]
159	
160	        if len(self.models) < original_count:
161	            self.save()
162	            return True
163	        return False
164	
165	    def update_model(self, name: str, updates: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
166	        """
167	        Update a model's attributes
168	
169	        Args:
170	            name: Name of the model to update
171	            updates: Dictionary of attributes to update
172	
173	        Returns:
174	            True if model was updated, False if not found
175	        """
176	        model = self.get_model(name)
177	        if not model:
178	            return False
179	
180	        # Update attributes
181	        for key, value in updates.items():
182	            if hasattr(model, key):
183	                setattr(model, key, value)
184	
185	        self.save()
186	        return True
187	
188	    def get_model(self, name: str) -> Optional[UserModel]:
189	        """
190	        Get a model by name
191	
192	        Args:
193	            name: Name of the model
194	
195	        Returns:
196	            UserModel instance or None if not found
197	        """
198	        for model in self.models:
199	            if model.name == name:
200	                return model
201	        return None
202	
203	    def get_model_by_id(self, model_id: str) -> Optional[UserModel]:
204	        """
205	        Get a model by its unique ID
206	
207	        Args:
208	            model_id: UUID of the model
209	
210	        Returns:
211	            UserModel instance or None if not found
212	        """
213	        for model in self.models:
214	            if model.id == model_id:
215	                return model
216	        return None
217	
218	    def list_models(self) -> List[UserModel]:
219	        """
220	        List all models
221	
222	        Returns:
223	            List of all UserModel instances
224	        """
225	        return self.models.copy()
226	
227	    def find_by_path(self, path: str) -> Optional[UserModel]:
228	        """
229	        Find a model by its path
230	
231	        Args:
232	            path: Model directory path
233	
234	        Returns:
235	            UserModel instance or None if not found
236	        """
237	        # Normalize paths for comparison
238	        search_path = str(Path(path).resolve())
239	
240	        for model in self.models:
241	            model_path = str(Path(model.path).resolve())
242	            if model_path == search_path:
243	                return model
244	        return None
245	
246	    def check_name_conflict(self, name: str, exclude_name: Optional[str] = None) -> bool:
247	        """
248	        Check if a name conflicts with existing models
249	
250	        Args:
251	            name: Name to check
252	            exclude_name: Optional name to exclude from check (for rename operations)
253	
254	        Returns:
255	            True if conflict exists, False otherwise
256	        """
257	        for model in self.models:
258	            if model.name == name and model.name != exclude_name:
259	                return True
260	        return False
261	
262	    def refresh_status(self) -> Dict[str, List[str]]:
263	        """
264	        Check all models and identify missing ones
265	
266	        Returns:
267	            Dictionary with 'valid' and 'missing' lists of model names
268	        """
269	        valid = []
270	        missing = []
271	
272	        for model in self.models:
273	            if model.path_exists():
274	                valid.append(model.name)
275	            else:
276	                missing.append(model.name)
277	
278	        return {"valid": valid, "missing": missing}
279	
280	    def get_model_count(self) -> int:
281	        """Get total number of registered models"""
282	        return len(self.models)
283	
284	    def suggest_name(self, base_name: str) -> str:
285	        """
286	        Suggest a unique name based on base_name
287	
288	        Args:
289	            base_name: Base name to derive from
290	
291	        Returns:
292	            A unique name (may have suffix like -2, -3 etc.)
293	        """
294	        if not self.check_name_conflict(base_name):
295	            return base_name
296	
297	        counter = 2
298	        while True:
299	            candidate = f"{base_name}-{counter}"
300	            if not self.check_name_conflict(candidate):
301	                return candidate
302	            counter += 1
303

1	"""
2	Model registry for kt-cli.
3	
4	Provides a registry of supported models with fuzzy matching capabilities.
5	"""
6	
7	import re
8	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
9	from pathlib import Path
10	from typing import Callable, Optional
11	
12	import yaml
13	
14	from kt_kernel.cli.config.settings import get_settings
15	
16	
17	@dataclass
18	class ModelInfo:
19	    """Information about a supported model."""

24	    type: str = "moe"  # moe, dense
25	    gpu_vram_gb: float = 0
26	    cpu_ram_gb: float = 0
27	    default_params: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
28	    description: str = ""
29	    description_zh: str = ""
30	    max_tensor_parallel_size: Optional[int] = None  # Maximum tensor parallel size for this model

32

47	        description_zh="DeepSeek V3-0324 685B MoE 模型（2025年3月，改进的基准测试）",
48	    ),
49	    ModelInfo(

51	        hf_repo="deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3.2",
52	        aliases=["deepseek-v3.2", "dsv3.2", "deepseek3.2", "v3.2"],
53	        type="moe",
54	        default_params={
55	            "kt-method": "FP8",

57	            "attention-backend": "flashinfer",

59	            "max-total-tokens": 100000,

64	            "served-model-name": "DeepSeek-V3.2",

66	        },
67	        description="DeepSeek V3.2 671B MoE model (latest)",
68	        description_zh="DeepSeek V3.2 671B MoE 模型（最新）",
69	    ),
70	    ModelInfo(
71	        name="DeepSeek-R1-0528",
72	        hf_repo="deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-0528",

173	
174	
175	class ModelRegistry:
176	    """Registry of supported models with fuzzy matching."""
177	
178	    def __init__(self):
179	        """Initialize the model registry."""
180	        self._models: dict[str, ModelInfo] = {}
181	        self._aliases: dict[str, str] = {}
182	        self._load_builtin_models()
183	        self._load_user_models()
184	
185	    def _load_builtin_models(self) -> None:
186	        """Load built-in models."""
187	        for model in BUILTIN_MODELS:
188	            self._register(model)
189	
190	    def _load_user_models(self) -> None:
191	        """Load user-defined models from config."""
192	        settings = get_settings()
193	        registry_file = settings.config_dir / "registry.yaml"
194	
195	        if registry_file.exists():
196	            try:
197	                with open(registry_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
198	                    data = yaml.safe_load(f) or {}
199	
200	                for name, info in data.get("models", {}).items():
201	                    model = ModelInfo(
202	                        name=name,
203	                        hf_repo=info.get("hf_repo", ""),
204	                        aliases=info.get("aliases", []),
205	                        type=info.get("type", "moe"),
206	                        gpu_vram_gb=info.get("gpu_vram_gb", 0),
207	                        cpu_ram_gb=info.get("cpu_ram_gb", 0),
208	                        default_params=info.get("default_params", {}),
209	                        description=info.get("description", ""),
210	                        description_zh=info.get("description_zh", ""),
211	                        max_tensor_parallel_size=info.get("max_tensor_parallel_size"),
212	                    )
213	                    self._register(model)
214	            except (yaml.YAMLError, OSError):
215	                pass
216	
217	    def _register(self, model: ModelInfo) -> None:
218	        """Register a model."""
219	        self._models[model.name.lower()] = model
220	
221	        # Register aliases
222	        for alias in model.aliases:
223	            self._aliases[alias.lower()] = model.name.lower()
224	
225	    def get(self, name: str) -> Optional[ModelInfo]:
226	        """Get a model by exact name or alias."""
227	        name_lower = name.lower()
228	
229	        # Check direct match
230	        if name_lower in self._models:
231	            return self._models[name_lower]
232	
233	        # Check aliases
234	        if name_lower in self._aliases:
235	            return self._models[self._aliases[name_lower]]
236	
237	        return None
238	
239	    def search(self, query: str, limit: int = 10) -> list[ModelInfo]:
240	        """Search for models using fuzzy matching.
241	
242	        Args:
243	            query: Search query
244	            limit: Maximum number of results
245	
246	        Returns:
247	            List of matching models, sorted by relevance
248	        """
249	        query_lower = query.lower()
250	        results: list[tuple[float, ModelInfo]] = []
251	
252	        for model in self._models.values():
253	            score = self._match_score(query_lower, model)
254	            if score > 0:
255	                results.append((score, model))
256	
257	        # Sort by score descending
258	        results.sort(key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
259	
260	        return [model for _, model in results[:limit]]
261	
262	    def _match_score(self, query: str, model: ModelInfo) -> float:
263	        """Calculate match score for a model.
264	
265	        Returns a score between 0 and 1, where 1 is an exact match.
266	        """
267	        # Check exact match
268	        if query == model.name.lower():
269	            return 1.0
270	
271	        # Check alias exact match
272	        for alias in model.aliases:
273	            if query == alias.lower():
274	                return 0.95
275	
276	        # Check if query is contained in name
277	        if query in model.name.lower():
278	            return 0.8
279	
280	        # Check if query is contained in aliases
281	        for alias in model.aliases:
282	            if query in alias.lower():
283	                return 0.7
284	
285	        # Check if query is contained in hf_repo
286	        if query in model.hf_repo.lower():
287	            return 0.6
288	
289	        # Fuzzy matching - check if all query parts are present
290	        query_parts = re.split(r"[-_.\s]", query)
291	        name_lower = model.name.lower()
292	
293	        matches = sum(1 for part in query_parts if part and part in name_lower)
294	        if matches > 0:
295	            return 0.5 * (matches / len(query_parts))
296	
297	        return 0.0
298	
299	    def list_all(self) -> list[ModelInfo]:
300	        """List all registered models."""
301	        return list(self._models.values())
302	
303	    def find_local_models(self, max_depth: int = 3) -> list[tuple[ModelInfo, Path]]:
304	        """Find models that are downloaded locally in any configured model path.
305	
306	        Args:
307	            max_depth: Maximum depth to search within each model path (default: 3)
308	
309	        Returns:
310	            List of (ModelInfo, path) tuples for local models
311	        """
312	        settings = get_settings()
313	        model_paths = settings.get_model_paths()
314	        results = []
315	
316	        for model in self._models.values():
317	            found = False
318	            # Search in all configured model directories
319	            for models_dir in model_paths:
320	                if not models_dir.exists():
321	                    continue
322	
323	                # Generate possible names to search for
324	                possible_names = [
325	                    model.name,
326	                    model.name.lower(),
327	                    model.hf_repo.split("/")[-1],
328	                    model.hf_repo.replace("/", "--"),
329	                ]
330	
331	                # Search recursively up to max_depth
332	                for depth in range(max_depth):
333	                    # Build glob pattern for current depth
334	                    # depth=0: direct children, depth=1: grandchildren, etc.
335	                    glob_pattern = "*" if depth > 0 else ""
336	                    for _ in range(depth):
337	                        glob_pattern = "*/" + glob_pattern if glob_pattern else "*"
338	
339	                    for name in possible_names:
340	                        if depth == 0:
341	                            # Direct children: models_dir / name
342	                            search_paths = [models_dir / name]
343	                        else:
344	                            # Nested: use rglob to find directories matching the name
345	                            search_paths = list(models_dir.rglob(name))
346	
347	                        for path in search_paths:
348	                            if path.exists() and (path / "config.json").exists():
349	                                results.append((model, path))
350	                                found = True
351	                                break
352	
353	                        if found:
354	                            break
355	
356	                    if found:
357	                        break
358	
359	                if found:
360	                    break
361	
362	        return results
363	
364	
365	# Global registry instance
366	_registry: Optional[ModelRegistry] = None
367	
368	
369	def get_registry() -> ModelRegistry:
370	    """Get the global model registry instance."""
371	    global _registry
372	    if _registry is None:
373	        _registry = ModelRegistry()
374	    return _registry
375	
376	
377	# ============================================================================
378	# Model-specific parameter computation functions
379	# ============================================================================
380	
381	
382	def compute_deepseek_v3_gpu_experts(tensor_parallel_size: int, vram_per_gpu_gb: float) -> int:
383	    per_gpu_gb = 16
384	    if vram_per_gpu_gb < per_gpu_gb:
385	        return int(0)
386	    total_vram = int(tensor_parallel_size * (vram_per_gpu_gb - per_gpu_gb))
387	
388	    return total_vram // 3
389	
390	
391	def compute_deepseek_v4_gpu_experts(tensor_parallel_size: int, vram_per_gpu_gb: float) -> int:
392	    """Compute kt-num-gpu-experts for DeepSeek-V4-Flash.
393	
394	    V4 uses MXFP4 experts (~0.5 bytes/param vs V3 FP8's 1 byte/param) so each GPU
395	    can hold ~2x more experts per VRAM unit than V3 at the same fragmentation.
396	    """
397	    per_gpu_gb = 16
398	    if vram_per_gpu_gb < per_gpu_gb:
399	        return 0
400	    total_vram = int(tensor_parallel_size * (vram_per_gpu_gb - per_gpu_gb))
401	    return total_vram * 2 // 3
402	
403	
404	def compute_kimi_k2_thinking_gpu_experts(tensor_parallel_size: int, vram_per_gpu_gb: float) -> int:
405	    """Compute kt-num-gpu-experts for Kimi K2 Thinking."""
406	    per_gpu_gb = 16
407	    if vram_per_gpu_gb < per_gpu_gb:
408	        return int(0)
409	    total_vram = int(tensor_parallel_size * (vram_per_gpu_gb - per_gpu_gb))
410	
411	    return total_vram * 2 // 3
412	
413	
414	def compute_minimax_m2_gpu_experts(tensor_parallel_size: int, vram_per_gpu_gb: float) -> int:
415	    """Compute kt-num-gpu-experts for MiniMax M2/M2.1."""
416	    per_gpu_gb = 16
417	    if vram_per_gpu_gb < per_gpu_gb:
418	        return int(0)
419	    total_vram = int(tensor_parallel_size * (vram_per_gpu_gb - per_gpu_gb))
420	
421	    return total_vram // 1
422	
423	
424	# Model name to computation function mapping
425	MODEL_COMPUTE_FUNCTIONS: dict[str, Callable[[int, float], int]] = {
426	    "DeepSeek-V3-0324": compute_deepseek_v3_gpu_experts,
427	    "DeepSeek-V3.2": compute_deepseek_v3_gpu_experts,  # Same as V3-0324
428	    "DeepSeek-R1-0528": compute_deepseek_v3_gpu_experts,  # Same as V3-0324
429	    "DeepSeek-V4-Flash": compute_deepseek_v4_gpu_experts,
430	    "Kimi-K2-Thinking": compute_kimi_k2_thinking_gpu_experts,
431	    "MiniMax-M2": compute_minimax_m2_gpu_experts,
432	    "MiniMax-M2.1": compute_minimax_m2_gpu_experts,  # Same as M2
433	}
434

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# Bash completion for kt command
3	# This is a static completion script that doesn't require Python startup
4	
5	_kt_completion() {
6	    local cur prev opts
7	    COMPREPLY=()
8	    cur="${COMP_WORDS[COMP_CWORD]}"
9	    prev="${COMP_WORDS[COMP_CWORD-1]}"
10	
11	    # Main commands
12	    local commands="version run chat quant edit bench microbench doctor model config sft"
13	
14	    # Global options
15	    local global_opts="--help --version"
16	
17	    # Handle subcommands
18	    case "${COMP_CWORD}" in
19	        1)
20	            # First argument: suggest commands and global options
21	            COMPREPLY=( $(compgen -W "${commands} ${global_opts}" -- ${cur}) )
22	            return 0
23	            ;;
24	        *)
25	            # Handle specific command options
26	            case "${COMP_WORDS[1]}" in
27	                run)
28	                    local run_opts="--host --port --gpu-experts --cpu-threads --tensor-parallel-size --kt-method --attention-backend --max-total-tokens --dry-run --help"
29	                    COMPREPLY=( $(compgen -W "${run_opts}" -- ${cur}) )
30	                    ;;
31	                chat)
32	                    local chat_opts="--host --port --model --temperature --max-tokens --system --save-history --no-save-history --history-file --stream --no-stream --help"
33	                    COMPREPLY=( $(compgen -W "${chat_opts}" -- ${cur}) )
34	                    ;;
35	                quant)
36	                    local quant_opts="--method --output --help"
37	                    COMPREPLY=( $(compgen -W "${quant_opts}" -- ${cur}) )
38	                    ;;
39	                edit)
40	                    local edit_opts="--help"
41	                    COMPREPLY=( $(compgen -W "${edit_opts}" -- ${cur}) )
42	                    ;;
43	                bench|microbench)
44	                    local bench_opts="--model --config --help"
45	                    COMPREPLY=( $(compgen -W "${bench_opts}" -- ${cur}) )
46	                    ;;
47	                doctor)
48	                    local doctor_opts="--verbose --help"
49	                    COMPREPLY=( $(compgen -W "${doctor_opts}" -- ${cur}) )
50	                    ;;

1	"""
2	Interactive configuration for kt run command - New Implementation.
3	
4	Provides step-by-step interactive configuration for running models.
5	"""
6	
7	from typing import Optional, List, Dict, Any, Tuple
8	from pathlib import Path
9	from rich.console import Console
10	from rich.table import Table
11	from rich.panel import Panel
12	from rich.prompt import Prompt, Confirm
13	from rich import box
14	import torch
15	
16	from kt_kernel.cli.i18n import t
17	from kt_kernel.cli.utils.input_validators import (
18	    prompt_int_with_retry,
19	    prompt_float_with_retry,
20	    prompt_choice_with_retry,
21	    prompt_int_list_with_retry,
22	)
23	
24	
25	console = Console()
26	
27	
28	def get_gpu_info() -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
29	    """Get real-time GPU information with free VRAM."""
30	    from kt_kernel.cli.utils.environment import detect_gpus
31	
32	    gpus = detect_gpus()
33	    gpu_info_list = []
34	
35	    for i, gpu in enumerate(gpus):
36	        total_vram_gb = gpu.vram_gb
37	        free_vram_gb = gpu.vram_gb  # Default fallback
38	
39	        # Try to get real-time free VRAM
40	        if torch.cuda.is_available() and i < torch.cuda.device_count():
41	            try:
42	                free_vram_bytes, total_vram_bytes = torch.cuda.mem_get_info(i)
43	                free_vram_gb = free_vram_bytes / (1024**3)
44	                total_vram_gb = total_vram_bytes / (1024**3)
45	            except Exception:
46	                pass  # Use fallback values
47	
48	        gpu_info_list.append(
49	            {
50	                "id": i,
51	                "name": gpu.name,
52	                "total_vram_gb": total_vram_gb,
53	                "free_vram_gb": free_vram_gb,
54	            }
55	        )
56	
57	    return gpu_info_list
58	
59	
60	def select_model() -> Optional[Any]:
61	    """Step 1: Select a safetensors MoE model.
62	
63	    Returns:
64	        Selected UserModel object or None if cancelled.
65	    """
66	    from kt_kernel.cli.utils.user_model_registry import UserModelRegistry
67	    from kt_kernel.cli.commands.model import is_amx_weights
68	
69	    registry = UserModelRegistry()
70	    all_models = registry.list_models()
71	
72	    # Filter: safetensors models only (exclude AMX and GGUF)
73	    # Then filter to only show MoE models (matching kt model list behavior)
74	    moe_models = []
75	    for model in all_models:
76	        if model.format == "safetensors" and model.path_exists():
77	            is_amx, _ = is_amx_weights(model.path)
78	            if not is_amx:
79	                # Only include MoE models (is_moe == True)
80	                # Also include models not yet analyzed (is_moe == None) for backwards compatibility
81	                if model.is_moe is True or model.is_moe is None:
82	                    moe_models.append(model)
83	
84	    if not moe_models:
85	        console.print(f"[yellow]{t('run_int_no_moe_models')}[/yellow]")
86	        console.print(f"  {t('run_int_add_models')}")
87	        console.print(f"  {t('run_int_list_all')}")
88	        return None
89	
90	    console.print()
91	    console.print(Panel(f"[bold cyan]{t('run_int_step1_title')}[/bold cyan]", expand=False))
92	    console.print()
93	
94	    # Display models using same format as kt model list
95	    from kt_kernel.cli.utils.model_scanner import format_size
96	    from kt_kernel.cli.commands.model import SHA256_STATUS_MAP
97	
98	    table = Table(box=box.ROUNDED, show_header=True, header_style="bold cyan")
99	    table.add_column("#", justify="right", style="cyan", no_wrap=True)
100	    table.add_column("Name", style="cyan", no_wrap=True)

1	"""
2	Environment detection utilities for kt-cli.
3	
4	Provides functions to detect:
5	- Virtual environment managers (conda, venv, uv, mamba)
6	- Python version and packages
7	- CUDA and GPU information
8	- System resources (CPU, RAM, disk)
9	"""
10	
11	import os
12	import platform
13	import shutil
14	import subprocess
15	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
16	from pathlib import Path
17	from typing import Optional
18	
19	
20	@dataclass
21	class EnvManager:
22	    """Information about an environment manager."""
23	
24	    name: str
25	    version: str
26	    path: str
27	
28	
29	@dataclass
30	class GPUInfo:
31	    """Information about a GPU."""
32	
33	    index: int
34	    name: str
35	    vram_gb: float
36	    cuda_capability: Optional[str] = None
37	
38	
39	@dataclass
40	class CPUInfo:
41	    """Information about the CPU."""
42	
43	    name: str
44	    cores: int
45	    threads: int
46	    numa_nodes: int
47	    instruction_sets: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # AVX, AVX2, AVX512, AMX, etc.
48	    numa_info: dict = field(default_factory=dict)  # node -> cpus mapping
49	
50	
51	@dataclass
52	class MemoryInfo:
53	    """Information about system memory."""
54	
55	    total_gb: float
56	    available_gb: float
57	    frequency_mhz: Optional[int] = None
58	    channels: Optional[int] = None
59	    type: Optional[str] = None  # DDR4, DDR5, etc.
60	
61	
62	@dataclass
63	class SystemInfo:
64	    """Complete system information."""
65	
66	    python_version: str
67	    platform: str
68	    cuda_version: Optional[str]
69	    gpus: list[GPUInfo]
70	    cpu: CPUInfo
71	    ram_gb: float
72	    env_managers: list[EnvManager]
73	
74	
75	def run_command(cmd: list[str], timeout: int = 10) -> Optional[str]:
76	    """Run a command and return its output, or None if it fails."""
77	    try:
78	        result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=timeout, check=False)
79	        if result.returncode == 0:
80	            return result.stdout.strip()
81	        return None
82	    except (subprocess.TimeoutExpired, FileNotFoundError, OSError):
83	        return None
84	
85	
86	def detect_env_managers() -> list[EnvManager]:
87	    """Detect available virtual environment managers."""
88	    managers = []
89	
90	    # Check conda
91	    conda_path = shutil.which("conda")
92	    if conda_path:
93	        version = run_command(["conda", "--version"])
94	        if version:
95	            # "conda 24.1.0" -> "24.1.0"
96	            version = version.split()[-1] if version else "unknown"
97	            managers.append(EnvManager(name="conda", version=version, path=conda_path))
98	
99	    # Check mamba
100	    mamba_path = shutil.which("mamba")
